item.title: 用 AI 拧紧螺丝 - 开发者倡导者揭穿“AI 可以取代开发者”神话的方法 | Turning the screw with AI - ways for developer advocates to debunk the "AI can replace developers" myth
item.description:
我们现在正处于科技界的动荡时期。 一方面，我们对人工智能的炒作如火如荼。 另一方面，公司正在大力“健康规模化” 。 我刚刚作为主要候选人经历了一轮工作面试，并写下了为什么我没有接受一些工作。 这让我意识到了一些事情。

开发商很担心

开发人员现在担心的是他们的工作是否安全，或者他们是否可以很容易地被 AI 取代。 他们还担心如何保持作为开发人员的相关性，而不是在一个似乎每小时都在涌现突破性创新的市场中落后。 此外，他们担心如何调整自己的工作预期，以免成为下一轮大规模裁员的一部分。

公司拧紧螺丝，让开发人员觉得他们很容易被替换

开发人员现在想工作的所有大公司都在展示他们的力量，试图剥夺开发人员长期以来一直享有的一些自由和特权。 在某种程度上，这很好，与其他工作相比，我们拥有多得离谱的津贴和自由。 但是，现在发生了一些烦人的事情。

公司通常实施激进的“重返办公室”政策，这有点像想要对员工有更多控制而不是信任他们的员工。 公司还不惜一切代价削减成本。 我见过重要的员工被解雇，因为他们在公司工作了很长时间，因此成本很高。

现在，在美国解雇一个有着长期业绩记录的人并不是什么大问题。 然而，在欧洲，你离职后得到的待遇和你得到多少个月的工资都与你在公司的花费有关。 因此，解雇一名工作 10 年以上的员工代价高得离谱。

一般来说，似乎有一种运动，即公司试图看看他们能在多大程度上削减额外津贴、薪水和福利，同时仍然拥有一流的员工。 通常，这与“人工智能正在改变一切”的说法密切相关，开发人员不再像过去那样被需要。

关于 Developer Advocacy 的快速提醒

在我的开发者倡导手册中，我将开发者倡导者定义为：

公司及其技术人员和外部开发人员之间的发言人、调解人和翻译。

因此，如果您是一家公司的开发人员倡导者，现在是时候加强第一部分了——确保公司与其开发人员之间的良好沟通。 并且——如果需要的话——在这些削减成本的谈判中成为他们的倡导者。

这与人工智能的威胁无关

由于 AI 创新，开发人员需要向公司证明自己的价值的说法是错误的。 这与颠覆性创新无关。 这是关于广告销售暴跌和股市遭受重创。 公司按人均盈利能力估值。 因此，当利润下降时 - 必须摇头。

埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的 Twitter 和其他人表明，你可以像 1920 年代的工厂主一样行事，人们不会抱怨，很多人都效仿了这个榜样。

还有一个问题是，我们生活在一个充满不确定性和衰退的普遍时期。 这就是民粹主义蓬勃发展的地方。 科技行业被保守派和极右翼发言人和政客称为“精英”。 一份只适合少数被宠爱的千禧一代的工作。 因此，科技界发生的任何坏事都被认为早该发生——我们终于也变得脆弱了。 当大流行来袭时，科技界是为数不多的仍然蓬勃发展的领域之一，因为人们需要使用互联网进行交流。 当 AI 作为一项新的大型运动首次出现时，许多人担心他们的工作。 现在轮到我们有同感了。

关于 AI 和开发人员工作的一些事实

  • 人工智能将抢走工作
  • 人工智能系统是提高人们效率的好方法，从而为公司节省资金
  • 开发人员应该了解人工智能
  • 大多数人工智能系统不适用于你的公司
  • 开放式法学硕士不擅长教授编码

让我们一点一点地研究它们，以及开发人员提倡的——或者实际上是在科技公司工作的任何人——应该对它们做些什么。

人工智能将抢走工作！

毫无疑问，一些工作将成为人工智能的牺牲品，因为它更容易获得并且计算能力已经赶上了它的需求。 然而，错误的说法是它将取代专业人士、专家甚至是高度创造性的工作。

生成式人工智能非常擅长创造一次性产品。 快速原型、概念证明、文章大纲需要在发布前进行事实检查和重写。

这不是新的。 长期以来，对可预测的一次性产品的需求一直持续存在，而这些产品实际上并不需要由熟练的开发人员或设计师来完成。 过去，这些工作也可以通过内容管理系统和模板来完成。 如果我们对自己诚实，几十年来，即使是开发人员和设计师也采取了捷径，重复使用其他人的代码，使用设计库或将我们购买和定制的组件放在一起。

科技公司试图推销的很酷的新事物是，我们可以通过提出正确的问题和人工智能来创建网络产品，它会为我们提供设计、为我们编写副本甚至创建代码。

前两个结果，编写通用副本和创建“经过验证的成功设计”令人担忧，因为它们将导致成千上万没有个人品牌或特征的相似产品。 这一直是设计师很苦恼的一个问题，但现在我们给人的错觉是一个导演，他可以通过提问和提炼来创造。 最终产品仍可能看起来像具有不同配色方案和不同徽标的同一产品。 使用通用设计和副本引导产品是开始的好方法，但这不是建立品牌的方法。

这会取代了解自己工作的设计师吗？ 不是真的，因为好的设计和写作比创建一个界面要多得多。 这会取代作家吗？ 并非如此，因为一位优秀的作家会在品牌和媒体限制下工作，而不是制作出听起来很聪明的副本。

最后一部分——人工智能还可以创建构建网络产品所需的所有代码——是一个谎言。 当然，您可以使用此副本生成创建此设计的代码，并可能添加一些基本交互。 但是一个真正的软件产品不是单一的设计。

它使自己与用户的需求保持一致，允许定制，人们可以根据自己的需要改变外观和感觉。 它还需要知道界面中的数据来自何处，以及无论输入设备如何与用户交互。 而且 - 更重要的是 - 它永远不应该对产品的文本内容做出任何假设，因为无论如何最终都会来自 CMS 并且必须支持多种语言。

这意味着不需要遵守这些质量标准甚至法律合规性需求的产品可以而且应该通过“所见即所得”的界面来完成。 如果人们乐于从一个人类问题开始并到达那里——所有的力量都给他们。

目前，这种 AI 正在从事一些让设计师和开发人员感到沮丧的工作。 我们都花了太多时间为产品展示制作原型、幻灯片和演示，而这些最终都被扔进了垃圾箱。 我们不得不处理数十轮反馈，人们希望“更大”或“更好的字体”。 如果这些人现在可以用这种要求惹恼 AI，那就太好了。

如果有的话，这是一个很好的机会，可以了解您在哪里为永远不会发布的产品应用开发人员和设计师资源，并将它们重定向到更好的原因。 因此，我们应该采取以下措施来应对这种威胁：

  • 分析我们公司的哪些工作可以通过 AI 实现自动化。
  • 为团队提供指导，让他们使用这些系统来创建开发团队可以根据需要进行扩充的材料。
  • 与高层管理人员沟通，将这些事情自动化可以释放开发人员资源，并提供这些团队完成的工作——而不是取代他们

在上下文中应用人工智能可以提高人们的效率并为公司节省资金

人工智能系统可以提供帮助的地方在于提高人们的工作效率。 这就是“上下文”部分的用武之地。人工智能可以在三个领域提供帮助：

  • 机器辅助代码完成
  • 协助协作和学习
  • 设计到代码转换

机器辅助代码补全是可以帮你写代码的“AI代码编辑器”。 它们不是新的，但它们正在变得更好。 这个想法是你写几行代码，编辑会建议剩下的。 这不仅与自动完成有关，还与上下文识别和样式识别有关。 大玩家是GitHub Copilot和Amazon CodeWhisperer 。 除了自动完成您的代码之外，这些工具还可以解释代码并具有精细控制，例如“使此代码更健壮”或“为此功能生成测试”。 这也意味着生成的代码会自动验证。

您使用这些工具的时间越长，结果就越相关。 我从一开始就一直在使用 Copilot，虽然它提供了我以前没有使用过的结果，但它确实模仿了我编写代码的方式。 这意味着它不仅是一种节省时间的工具，也是一种学习的工具。 并且生成的代码符合我使用的标准。 这在团队中可能非常强大，您可以使用这样的编辑器来执行编码标准，并让编辑器生成符合该标准的代码，而无需进行数月的讨论。

另一个有趣的想法是Copilot for pull requests ，它是 pull requests 的自动完成。 您告诉它编写摘要和分步说明，它使用拉取请求中的代码来创建这些内容。 这可以节省大量时间并确保您的拉取请求始终是最新的。 没有可读或可操作的拉取请求是许多公司的一个大问题，这可能是解决它的好方法。

已经有证据表明，使用这样的工具对提高团队效率非常有用。 论文The Effect of AI-Assisted Code Completion on Software Development Productivity描述了一项研究，其中一组开发人员使用了这样的工具，并且比没有使用这种工具的团队效率高 40%。

在设计方面，有一些有趣的 Figma 插件和其他可用的设计套件。 您可以自行决定如何提高您团队的效率：

  • 确定您的公司可以在上下文中实施哪些工具，并指导团队如何使用它们。
  • 在市场上测试和验证系统。

开发人员应该了解 AI

毫无疑问，开发人员应该了解人工智能并深入了解这些看似可以取代它们的“神奇”系统是如何工作的。 这意味着公司现在应该加大力度让员工了解“人工智能”的工作原理和局限性。 五年前，我为 Skillshare 写了一门名为“揭秘人工智能：理解机器学习”的课程，虽然它当然不是最新的变化，但它仍然很好地让人们了解“思考机器”可以做什么以及在哪里限制、伦理问题和技术问题是。

大公司也明白，工程师需要了解大型 AI 系统的内部工作原理。 例如，DeepLearning.ai 有一个ChatGPT Prompt Engineering for Developers课程，解释了如何提出正确的问题，而谷歌有一个完整深入的Generative AI 学习路径。 我们现在应该做的事情是：

  • 收集最新的材料和课程并在团队中分发
  • 与管理层谈判以获得人工智能事务官方培训的预算

并非所有人工智能系统都适用于您的公司

虽然很高兴看到人们使用 AI 做的所有事情，但重要的是要了解并非所有这些系统都适用于您的公司。 有趣的是，即使像谷歌和亚马逊这样的大公司也在内部警告不要使用聊天机器人，因为他们担心数据泄露和系统被用于恶意目的。 所以这里似乎有一个巨大的“照我说的做，不照我做的”。

公司需要问问自己，他们是否可以允许 AI 系统为他们的代码库编制索引，是否甚至可以访问内部设置和依赖项，以及他们是否乐于使用他们的代码库来训练 AI 系统。 其他问题与使用和合规性有关。 您可以在不知道其许可或不知道其来源的情况下使用代码吗？ 如何确保代码质量和安全性？

我们现在需要采取的步骤是：

  • 带领团队成员审查不同的工具并将其展示给公司，然后讨论这些工具是否适用。
  • 评估和审查不同的 AI 产品，并与领导和高层管理人员合作，看看可以应用什么。

我们作为开发人员的职业发生了变化

我们现在必须应对的最大变化是，如果我们进入一个以人工智能生成内容为第一步的世界，这将影响我们的职业生涯。 我们需要意识到，我们不再是编写代码的人，而是审查代码的人。 这在传统上是高级开发人员的工作，但现在是所有开发人员的工作。 我们需要改变我们的职业目标和期望。 初级开发人员的角色变得越来越小，人们在评估和审查技能方面需要更快地提升。 高级开发人员需要对初级开发人员进行更多的指导和培训，以发现生成的代码中的问题，而不是编写代码或审查手写代码。 从本质上讲，我们正在将两个职业级别合二为一。 这可能是一件好事，从长远来看可以为公司节省资金。

为了实现这一点，我们需要：

  • 与管理层和首席工程师一起制定公司战略，使审查代码成为一项核心技能。
  • 相应地重新评估层次结构和职业目标和期望。

开放式法学硕士不擅长教授代码

有数百个演示和视频，人们可以在其中使用 ChatGPT 编写代码、构建网站或编写游戏。 这些是一个很好的灵感，我完全支持人们以有趣和引人入胜的方式学习代码。 问题是这并没有教我们如何编程。 它向我们展示了如何使用工具来生成代码。 这是一个巨大的差异。 当然，有些工具也带有代码解释，但没有一个能告诉你代码的缺点。 它们都没有告诉您有关安全问题或如何提高代码性能的信息。 他们都没有告诉您代码的许可证，或者使用它是否合法。

这不是什么新鲜事。 多年来，开发人员一直在从论坛和教程中复制和粘贴他们不理解的代码，更改一些数字，当没有任何问题时，将其提交给项目。 然而，这也是一件非常危险的事情。

法学硕士非常适合让事情听起来很容易做。 他们擅长让人们觉得他们是伟大的开发者。 但是伟大的开发人员知道他们编写的代码为何有效，而不仅仅是编写有效的代码。 更糟糕的是，不良行为者已经以此为契机让开发人员使用恶意和不安全的代码。 在某些情况下，ChatGPT 生成的代码建议包拼写错误或不存在，并且在安装时安装了恶意软件。

解决方法是要么设置自己的 ChatGPT 类产品，要么限制开发人员可以使用的资源并立即验证生成的代码。 我最喜欢的事情之一是它允许您自定义您希望系统显示的信息级别。 因此，它可用于向初级开发人员传授基础知识，并提醒高级开发人员他们可能已经忘记的语法。

如果在您的编辑环境中使用，这也意味着将自动检查使用这些系统生成的代码是否存在语法错误和问题。

为了使用 LLM 教授内部开发人员，您需要：

  • 为生成的代码定义并与团队商定“代码卫生”计划。
  • 将 LLM 的结果限制在经过验证的来源和内部代码存储库中。
  • 与管理层合作，利用节省的时间实施最佳实践培训，学习如何验证生成的代码。

现在是将软件开发作为一门手艺而奋斗的时候了

这些是抵消“我们不需要开发人员，我们有 AI”论点的一些方法，同时拥抱这些新的技术工作方式。 我相信还有更多，我很想听听您对此的看法。 总的来说，我认为我们现在面临着一项艰巨的任务，即捍卫软件开发是一门手艺而不是一种商品。 当然，我们可以在没有任何设计师、作家或开发人员参与的情况下创造很多东西。 但是就像您可以靠微波炉生存一样，这与家常菜不同。



原文:

We are in times of turmoil in the tech world right now. On the one side, we have the AI hype in full swing. On the other, companies are heavily “healthsizing”. I just went through a round of job interviews as a prime candidate and wrote up why I didn’t take some jobs. It made me realise a few things.

Developers are worried

Things that developers worry about right now are if their jobs are safe or if they could be easily replaced by AI. They also worry about how to stay relevant as developers and not fall behind in a market that seems to churn out ground-breaking innovations by the hour. Furthermore, they worry how they should adjust their job expectations to not be part of the next round of mass layoffs.

Companies turn the screw and make developers feel that they are easily replaceable

All the big companies that developers want to work at right now flex their muscles trying to take away some of the freedoms and perks developers have been enjoying for a long time. To a degree, this is fine, as compared to other jobs we had a ridiculous amount of perks and freedoms. However, there are a few annoying things happening right now.

Companies in general implement an aggressive “back to office” policy which smacks of wanting to have more control over people rather than trusting their employees. Companies also cut costs at all cost. I've seen vital employees being laid off because they've been with the company for a long time and thus cost a lot.

Now, laying someone off in the US with a long track record isn't that much of an issue. In Europe, however, the package you get for leaving and how many more months you get salary is connected to the amount you spent at the company. So, laying off a 10+ years employee is ridiculously expensive.

Generally, there seems to be a movement that companies try to see how far they can cut perks, salaries and benefits and yet still have top-notch employees. Often this goes hand in hand with a narrative that "AI is changing everything" and developers are not as needed as they used to be.

A quick reminder about Developer Advocacy

In my developer advocacy handbook, I defined a developer advocate as:

A spokesperson, mediator and translator between a company and its technical staff and outside developers.

So, if you are a developer advocate in a company, it is now the time to ramp up on first part – ensuring a good communication between the company and its developers. And - if need be - be their advocate in these cost-cutting negotiations.

This isn’t about the threat of AI

The argument that developers need to prove their worth to a company because of AI innovation is bogus. This isn't about disruptive innovation. It is about ad sales plumeting and the stock market taking a punch. Companies are valued at profitability per head. So when profits go down - heads must roll.

Elon Musk with Twitter and others showed that you can act like a factory owner in the 1920s and people won’t complain and many people follow that example.

There's also the problem that that we live in a general time of uncertainty and decline. And this is where populism thrives. The technology sector has been called by conservative and far-right spokespeople and politicians an "elite". A job for only a chosen few, pampered millennials. So anything bad happening to the tech world is considered long overdue - we finally are also vulnerable. When the pandemic hit, the tech world was one of the few that still thrived as people needed to use the internet to communicate. When AI as a large new movement first came around, many people worried about their jobs. Now it is our turn to feel the same.

Some facts about AI and developer jobs

  • AI will take jobs
  • AI systems are a great way to make people more effective, thus saving the company money
  • Developers should be knowledgeable about AI
  • Most AI systems won’t be applicable to your company
  • AI means a big change to developer careers
  • Open LLMs are bad at teaching coding

Let’s go through them bit by bit and what developer advocates - or indeed anyone working in tech companies - should do about them.

AI will take jobs!

There is no doubt that some jobs will fall prey to AI now that it is much more readily available and computing power has caught up with its demands. The false narrative though is that it will replace specialist humans, experts and even highly creative jobs.

Generative AI is great at creating throwaway products. Quick prototypes, proof of concepts, an outline for an article that will need to get fact checked and re-written before publication.

This isn't new. There has been an ongoing demand for predictable, throw-away products for quite a while that don't really need to be done by a skilled developer or designer. In the past, these jobs could also be done with a content management system and a template. If we are honest with ourselves, even developers and designers have taken shortcuts for decades, re-using other people's code, working with design libraries or putting together components we bought and customised.

The cool new thing tech companies try to sell is that we can create a web product by asking the right questions and an AI and it will give us designs, write copy for us and even create the code.

The first two results, writing generic copy and creating a "proven successful design" are worrying as they will result in thousands of look-alike products with no personal branding or character. This has been a worry of designers for quite a while now, but now we give people the illusion of being a director who can create by asking questions and refining them. The end products will still likely look like the same product with a different colour scheme and a different logo. Bootstrapping a product with a generic design and copy is a great way to get started, but it is not a way to build a brand.

Will this replace designers who know their job? Not really as there is a lot more to good design and writing than creating one interface. Will this replace writers? Not really, as a good writer works with the brand and the media limitations instead of churning out clever sounding copy.

The last part - that AI can also create all the code needed to build a web product - is a lie. Sure, you can generate code that creates this design with this copy and maybe add some basic interaction. But a real software product isn't one design.

It aligns itself with the needs of the user, allows for customisation and people to change the look and feel to their needs. It also needs to know where the data in the interface comes from and what to do with user interaction regardless of input device. And - even more importantly - it should never make any assumptions about the text content of the product, as that will eventually come from a CMS anyways and have to support multiple languages.

This means though that products that don't need to adhere to these quality standards or even legal compliance needs can and should be done with a "what you see is what you get" interface. And if people are happy to start with a human question to get there - all power to them.

This kind of AI is currently taking jobs that have been nothing but frustrating to designers and developers. We all have spent far too many hours building prototypes, slideware and demos for product shows that were eventually for the bin. And we had to deal with dozens of rounds of feedback that people want "this bigger" or "a nicer font". If these people can now annoy an AI with this kind of demands - great.

If anything, this is a good opportunity to see where you apply developer and designer resources for products that will never be released anyway and redirect them to better causes. So here's what we should do to deal with this threat:

  • Analyse what part of our company’s work could be automated with AI.
  • Provide coaching for teams to use these systems to create materials that can be augmented by the development team if needed.
  • Communicate with upper management that automating these things frees developer resources and provide work to be done by these teams - not replace them

Applying AI in context makes people more effective and saves the company money

Where AI systems can help is by making people more effective. This is where the "in context" part comes in. There are three areas where AI can help:

  • Machine Aided Code completion
  • Aiding Collaboration and Learning
  • Design to code conversion

Machine aided code completion are "AI code editors" that can help you write code. They are not new, but they are getting better. The idea is that you write a few lines of code and the editor will suggest the rest. This is not just about auto-completion, but about context recognition and style recognition. The big players are GitHub Copilot and Amazon CodeWhisperer. In addition to auto-completing your code, these tools can also explain code and have granular controls like "make this code more robust" or "generate tests for this function". GitHub Copilot also offers a ChatBot interface that will run in the context of the currently open project, thus giving your developers the power of a chat client in their code editor. This also means that generated code is automatically validated.

The longer you use these tools, the more relevant the results get. I've been using Copilot since its beginning and whilst it offers me results I didn't use before, it does mimic the way I write code. This means that it is not just a tool to save time, but also a tool to learn from. And that the generated code adheres to a standard that I use. This could be incredibly powerful in a team, where you could use an editor like this to enforce a coding standard and have the editor generate code that adheres to it without months of discussion.

Another interesting idea is Copilot for pull requests which is an autocompletion for pull requests. You tell it to write a summary and a step-by-step instruction and it takes the code in the pull request to create those. This saves a lot of time and ensures that your pull requests are always up-to-date. Having no readable or actionable pull requests is a big problem in many companies and this could be a great way to solve it.

There is already evidence that using a tool like this is great for efficiency of teams. The paper The Effect of AI-Assisted Code Completion on Software Development Productivity describes a study where a team of developers used a tool like this and were 40% more efficient than a team that didn't.

On the design front, there are some interesting plugins for Figma and other design suites available. Locofy generates React and other framework components from your designs and helps with automated tagging of those. Deque's Design tools give designers an idea what a certain component should be like in code and how to make them accessible.

It is up to you to find out how this could boost the efficiency of your teams:

  • Determine what tools your company could implement in-context and coach the teams how to use them.
  • Test out and validate systems out in the market.

Developers should know about AI

There is no doubt that developers should know about AI and get an in-depth look at how these "magical" systems work that can seemingly replace them. This means that companies should now ramp up on getting their employees to understand the workings and limitations of "AI". Five years ago I wrote a course for Skillshare called Demystifying Artificial Intelligence: Understanding Machine Learning and whilst it is of course not up-to-date with current changes it still does a good job making people understand what "thinking machines" can do and where the limits, ethical issues and technical problems are.

The big players also understand that there is a need for engineers to know the inner workings of the big AI systems. DeepLearning.ai for example has a ChatGPT Prompt Engineering for Developers course that explains how to ask the right questions and Google has a full in-depth Generative AI learning path. Microsoft has a Quickstart Guide how to build a ChatBot for your own data.

The things we should be doing now are:

  • Collect up-to-date materials and courses and distribute them in the team
  • Negotiate with management to get a budget for official training on AI matters

Not all AI systems will work for your company

Whilst it is great to see all the things people do with AI, it is important to understand that not all of these systems will work for your company. Interestingly enough, even the big players like Google and Amazon warn internally about using chatbots as they are worried about data leaks and the systems being used for malicious purposes. So it seems there is a huge "Do as I say, not do as I do" going on here.

Companies need to question themselves if they can allow AI systems to index their codebase, if the internal setup and dependencies can even be accessed and if they are happy to have their codebase be used to train an AI system. Other questions are about usage and compliance. Can you use code without knowing its license or knowing where it came from? How does AI generated code fit into your review and compliance processes? How can you ensure code quality and security?

Steps we need to take now are:

  • Lead team members to review different tools and present them to the company followed by a discussion if the tools are applicable.
  • Assess and review different AI offerings and work with leads and upper management to see what can be applied.

Our careers as developers change

The biggest change we have to deal with right now is that if we get into a world where content generation via AI is the first step, that this affects our careers. We need to be aware that we are not the ones writing the code anymore, but we are the ones reviewing it. This traditionally is the job of a senior developer, but it is now the job of all developers. We need to change our career goals and expectations. The role of a junior developer becomes a lot smaller and people need to ramp up faster when it comes to their assessing and reviewing skills. And senior developers need to do more coaching and training of juniors to spot issues in generated code rather than writing code or doing reviews of hand-written code. In essence, we are merging two career levels into one. This could be a good thing, and it could save the company money in the long run.

To make this happen we need to:

  • Work with management and lead engineers to define a company strategy to make reviewing code a core skill.
  • Re-assess hierarchy and career goals and expectations accordingly.

Open LLMs are bad at teaching code

There are hundreds of demos and videos where people use ChatGPT to write code, build a web site or write a game. These are a great inspiration and I am all for people learning about code in a playful and engaging manner. The problem is that this doesn't teach us how to program. It shows us how to use a tool to generate code. This is a huge difference. Granted, some tools also come with code explanations, but none of them tell you about shortcomings of the code. None of them tell you about security issues or how to make the code more performant. None of them tell you about the license of the code or if it is even legal to use it.

This is nothing new. Developers have been copying and pasting code they don't understand from forums and tutorials for ages, changed some numbers around and when nothing blew up, submitted it to the project. This, however, was also an incredibly dangerous thing to do.

LLMs are great to make things sound like they are easy to do. They are good at making people feel like they are great developers. But great developers know why the code they write works, not just write code that works. Even worse, bad actors already use this as an opportunity to make developers use malicious and insecure code. There have been instances where ChatGPT generated code proposed packages that were misspelled or didn't exist and when installed, they installed malware.

The way around that is to either set up your own ChatGPT like product or limit the sources your developers can use and immediately validate the generated code. GitHub CoPilot for docs is an interesting idea to give you a ChatGPT like interface but limit it to official documentation of certain projects.

One of my favourite things about it is that it allows you to customise the level of information you want the system to display. Thus it can be used to teach a junior developer the basics, as well as remind senior developers about syntax they may have forgotten.

If used inside your editing environment, this also means that code generated with these systems will automatically be checked for syntax errors and issues.

In order to use LLMs to teach internal developers, you need to:

  • Define and agree a “code hygiene” plan with the team for generated code.
  • Limit results of LLMs to validated sources and internal code repositories.
  • Work with management to use saved time to implement best practice trainings to learn how to validate generated code.

This is the time to fight for software development as a craft

These are some ways to counteract the "we don't need developers, we have AI" argument whilst embracing these new ways of working in tech. I am sure there are many more and I'd love to hear your thoughts on this. Generally I think we now have the big task ahead of us to defend software development as a craft rather than a commodity. Sure, we can create a lot of stuff without any designer, writer or developer involved. But much like you can survive on microwave dishes, it is not the same as a home cooked meal.
item.author: codepo8

item.title: 每个开发人员都需要了解的十大网站 | Top 10 Websites Every Developer Needs to Know About
item.description:
在本文中，我将分享一些可以为您的日常工作提供帮助的最佳网站。 这些网站一直是我获取所有信息的首选资源。 这些网站将节省您的时间并提高您的工作效率。 所以系好安全带，喝杯咖啡，让我们深入了解每位开发人员都需要了解的 10 大网站！

目录

纪录片

该工具旨在通过在几秒钟内快速提供准确且相关的搜索结果来节省开发人员的宝贵时间并提高他们的工作效率。

输入查询后，Documatic 会快速从代码库中提取相关代码块，让您轻松找到所需的信息

转换工具

当我必须转换任何东西时，它真的节省了我的时间。

转换

超过 309 种不同的文档、图像、电子表格、电子书、档案、演示文稿、音频和视频格式。 例如 PNG 到 JPEG、SVG 到 PNG、PNG 到 ICO 等等。

删除bg

无论您是从事图形设计、照片编辑还是涉及图像的任何其他项目。 我已经用过这个工具很多次了，我什至不记得了。

伊姆古尔

您可以通过发布公共评论或发送私人消息、GIF 或表情符号与其他 Imgur 用户交流。 但我使用最多的功能是托管图像，您可以通过拖放或使用其 API 来发布图像。 它非常方便。

放大镜

它是一个一体化的 AI 工具包，可帮助您增强和提升图像质量。 在不损失质量的情况下提高图像分辨率。

代码美化

除此功能外，它还支持一些转换器，例如 Image 到 base64，不仅如此，它还具有大量功能，如下图所示：

韦塞尔

它是一个云平台，可以自动化构建无服务器 Web 应用程序的开发和部署过程。 它提供无服务器功能、静态站点托管、持续部署、自定义域和 SSL 以及团队协作等功能。 它有免费套餐和更高级功能的付费计划，并被许多流行的网站和 Web 应用程序使用。

扎皮尔

它旨在通过自动化数据输入、通知、文件上传等任务来简化重复性任务和工作流程。 它可以节省您的时间。 如果您还没有尝试过，那么我强烈建议您尝试一下。

雷.so

您可以通过从多种语法颜色中进行选择、决定是否隐藏背景以及在深色和浅色窗口模式之间切换来自定义您的作品。 这是以真正美丽的方式展示您的代码的完美方式。

包起来

在本文中，我介绍了我的一些最常用的网站，每个开发人员都应该了解这些网站。 这些工具可以增强开发人员的工作流程和生产力。 无论您是经验丰富的开发人员还是刚刚起步，利用正确的资源都可以帮助您节省时间。

我希望你觉得这篇文章有用。 别忘了❤️这篇文章。 下一篇再见。 与此同时，你可以在这里关注我：



原文:

In this article, I'll share some of the best websites that can help you in your day-to-day work. These sites have been my go-to resources for everything. These websites will save you time and improve your productivity. So buckle up, grab a cup of coffee, and let's dive into the top 10 websites every developer needs to know about!

Table of Contents

Documatic

Documatic is a highly efficient search engine tool created to help developers search their codebase for specific code snippets, functions, methods and other relevant information with ease. This tool is designed to save developers valuable time and increase their productivity by quickly providing accurate and relevant search results within seconds.

Documatic is a code search tool with a natural language query feature that simplifies codebase searches for both expert and novice developers. Once you enter your query, Documatic quickly fetches the relevant code block from the codebase, making it easy to find the information you need

Transform.tools

transform.tools is a website that lets you transform mostly everything such as HTML to JSX, JavaScript to JSON, CSS to JS Objects and many more. It really saved my time when I have to transform anything.

Convertio

Convertio - Easy tool to convert files online. More than 309 different documents, images, spreadsheets, ebooks, archives, presentations, audio and video formats. Such as PNG to JPEG, SVG to PNG, PNG to ICO and many others.

Removebg

Removebg is an amazing tool that allows you to easily remove the background of any image.RemoveBG instantly detects the subject of the image and removes the background, leaving you with a transparent PNG image that you can easily utilize in your projects. Whether you're working on graphic design, photo editing, or any other project that involves images. I have used this tool so many times I don't even remember.

Imgur

Imgur is an online content hosting site where you can view and share content such as images, GIFs, memes, videos and reviews. You can communicate with other Imgur users by posting public comments or sending private messages, GIFs or emojis. But the feature I used the most is hosting the images and you can post the images by drag and drop or use its API to do that. It comes in very handy.

Imglarger

AI Image Enlarger allows you to scale images up to 800% and enhance photos without losing quality, which is especially useful for photographers and graphics. It's an all-in-one AI toolkit that helps you enhance and upscale images. Increases image resolution without losing quality.

Code Beautify

CodeBeautify is an online Code Beautifier and Code Formatter that allows you to beautify your source code. Along with this feature, it also supports some converters such as Image to base64, not only this it has tons of functionality as shown in the following image:

Vercel

Vercel is the platform for front-end developers, providing the speed and reliability innovators need to create at the moment of inspiration. it is a cloud platform that automates the development and deployment process for building serverless web applications. It provides features such as serverless functions, static site hosting, continuous deployment, custom domains and SSL, and team collaboration. It has a free tier and paid plans for more advanced features and is used by many popular websites and web applications.

Zapier

Zapier is an online automation tool that connects and moves data between different web applications through simple workflows, called Zaps. It aims to simplify and streamline repetitive tasks and workflows, by automating tasks such as data entry, notifications, file uploads, and more. Zapier has over 1,500 web app integrations, including popular services like Gmail, Slack, Trello, Salesforce, and many more. It saves you time. If you haven't tried this then I highly recommend you to try this.

ray.so

ray.so transforms your code into visually stunning images with just a few clicks! You can customize your artwork by selecting from a wide variety of syntax colors, deciding whether or not to hide the background and switching between dark and light window modes. It's the perfect way to showcase your code in a truly beautiful way.

Wrapping up

In this article, I've covered some of the most used websites of mine that every developer should know about. These tools can enhance a developer's workflow and productivity. Whether you are an experienced developer or just starting out, utilizing the right resources can help you save time.

I hope you found this article useful. Don't forget to ❤️ the article. I'll see you in the next one. In the meantime you can follow me here:
item.author: j471n

item.title: 如何使用 ZenStack 在 10 分钟内使用 100 行代码构建可扩展的 SaaS 后端 | How To Build a Scalable SaaS Backend in 10 Minutes With 100 Lines of Code Using ZenStack
item.description:
在我之前的公司参与过四种商业 SaaS 产品的开发后，我开始意识到与典型的消费产品相比，它会产生大量的复杂性。 在这些复杂性中，一个突出的领域是权限控制和访问策略的复杂领域。

除了个人用户权限外，还需要在组织级别管理权限，更不用说用户未注意到的关键租户隔离方面。 无论您选择 RBAC 还是 ABAC 访问控制机制，系统的复杂性都会随着其功能的增加而增加。 因此，添加新功能所需的时间逐渐变慢，类似于 Martin Fowler 的设计耐力假设中蓝线所描绘的下降趋势：

时间并不是最糟糕的事情，因为我亲眼目睹了添加新功能会破坏现有功能的实例。 这在 B2B SaaS 领域尤其成问题，此类问题可能会导致致命后果。

门禁代码散乱

促成这一挑战的一个突出因素是代码库中访问控制处理的分散性。 一些访问控制逻辑集中在特定的组件中，例如中间件或数据库的 RLS（行级安全性），而其他的则在单独的组件中实现。 因此，即使是看似简单的更改也需要对整个代码库及其复杂性有深入的了解。

开发人员不仅必须小心不要无意中破坏现有功能，而且他们还必须确定实施更改的最佳位置，而不会引入潜在问题。

如果有一种方法可以将所有访问控制逻辑整合到一个集中位置会怎样？

单一事实来源

它在 Prisma 之上使用声明式数据模型，添加访问策略和验证规则，它将自动为您生成 RESTful 或 tRPC API。

使用 ZenStack 的 SaaS 项目

这是您可以开始的 SaaS 后端项目：

特征

  • 多租户
  • 软删除
  • 群组分享

数据模型

在schema.zmodel,有4个模型，它们的关系如下：

  • 组织是顶级租户。 一个用户可以属于多个组织和组
  • 一个帖子属于一个用户，可以属于多个组。

权限

让我们看看 Post 的所有权限以及如何使用 ZenStack 的访问策略来表达它们。

所有者必须设置为当前用户，组织必须设置为当前用户所属的组织。

 @@allow('create', owner == auth() && org.members?[this == auth()])

  • 更新

    只有所有者可以更新它，不允许更改组织或所有者

    @@allow('update', owner == auth() && org.future().members?[this == auth()] && future().owner == owner)

  • 读

    • 允许所有者阅读

      @@allow('read', owner == auth())

    • 允许组织成员阅读它（如果它是公开的）

       @@allow('read', isPublic && org.members?[this == auth()])

    • 允许小组成员阅读

      @@allow('read', groups?[users?[id == auth().id]])

  • 删除

    • 如果isDeleted为真，则记录被视为已删除，即软删除。

       @@deny('all', isDeleted == true)

您可以看到完整的数据模型以及上面定义的访问策略

abstract model organizationBaseEntity { id String @id @default(uuid()) createdAt DateTime @default(now()) updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt isDeleted Boolean @default(false) @omit isPublic Boolean @default(false) owner User @relation(fields: [ownerId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade) ownerId String org Organization @relation(fields: [orgId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade) orgId String groups Group[] // when create, owner must be set to current user, and user must be in the organization @@allow('create', owner == auth() && org.members?[this == auth()]) // only the owner can update it and is not allowed to change the owner @@allow('update', owner == auth() && org.members?[this == auth()] && future().owner == owner) // allow owner to read @@allow('read', owner == auth()) // allow shared group members to read it @@allow('read', groups?[users?[this == auth()]]) // allow organization to access if public @@allow('read', isPublic && org.members?[this == auth()]) // can not be read if deleted @@deny('all', isDeleted == true) } model Post extends organizationBaseEntity { title String content String }

模型继承

您可能好奇为什么这些规则是在抽象的organizationBaseEntity模型而不是具体的Post模型中定义的。 这就是为什么我说它是可扩展的。 借助ZenStack的模型继承能力，所有常用的权限都可以在抽象的基础模型中方便的处理。

考虑新聘用的开发人员需要添加新的ToDo模型的场景。 他可以通过简单地扩展organizationBaseEntity毫不费力地实现这一点：

 model ToDo extends organizationBaseEntity { name String isCompleted Boolean @default(false) }

所有多租户、软删除和共享功能都将自动运行。 此外，如果ToDo需要任何专门的访问控制逻辑，例如允许共享个人更新它，您可以毫不费力地在ToDo模型中添加相应的策略规则，而不必担心破坏现有功能：

 @@allow('update', groups?[users?[this== auth()]] )

我需要写多少 Typescript/JavaScript 代码

因此，您已经将模式作为您的业务模型和访问控制的唯一真实来源。 您可能希望看到接下来需要编写的 Typescript/Javascript 代码。 这是令人兴奋的部分：

就这样。 您几乎不需要编写任何 TS/JS 代码。 访问控制将由我们之前讨论的访问策略生成的代码在幕后无缝处理。

虽然您可能会在index.ts中观察到几行代码，但它主要用于将自动生成的 RESTful API 安装到 Express.js（它与所有主要的 Node.js 框架无缝集成）并启动应用程序。

让我们一起玩

我创建了示例数据供您试用。 您可以运行以下命令为其播种：

数据如下：

所以在 Prisma 团队中，每个用户都创建了一个帖子：

  • 关注 Twitter是公开的，以便 Robin、Bryan 和 Gavin 可以看到

您可以简单地调用 Post 端点来查看模拟不同用户的结果：

 curl -H "X-USER-ID: robin@prisma.io" localhost:3000/api/post

在现实世界中，您应该使用更安全的方式来传递 ID，例如 JWT 令牌。

根据示例数据，每个用户应该看到从 0 到 3 的不同数量的帖子。

软删除

既然是软删除，实际操作就是更新isDeleted为true。 让我们通过运行以下命令来删除 Robin 的“Join Salck”帖子：

 curl -X PUT \ -H "X-USER-ID: robin@prisma.io" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"data":{ "type":"post", "attributes":{ "isDeleted": true } } }'\ localhost:3000/api/post/slack

之后，如果您再次尝试访问 Post 端点，结果将不再包含“加入 Slack”帖子。 如果您对它在 hook 下的工作原理感兴趣，请查看另一篇文章：

最后的

如果您想了解他们采用 ZenStack 的经验，欢迎您加入我们的Discord 。 有关其他信息和示例，请查看我们的官方网站。



原文:

Having been involved in the development of four commercial SaaS products at my previous company, I've come to realize the multitude of complexities that arise compared to typical consumer products. Among these complexities, one prominent area lies in the intricate realm of permission control and access policies.

In addition to individual user permissions, there is also a need to manage permissions at the organizational level, not to mention the crucial aspect of tenant isolation that goes unnoticed by users. Regardless of whether you opt for RBAC or ABAC access control mechanisms, the system's complexity increases alongside its functionality. Consequently, the time required to add new features gradually slows down, akin to the declining trend depicted by the blue line in Martin Fowler’s Design Stamina Hypothesis:

Time is not the worst thing, as I've witnessed instances where adding new features can break existing ones. This becomes particularly problematic in the B2B SaaS realm, where such issues can have fatal consequences.

Access control code is scattered

One prominent factor contributing to this challenge is the scattered nature of access control handling within the code base. Some access control logic is centralized in specific components, such as middleware or the database's RLS (Row-Level Security), while others are implemented within individual components. As a result, even seemingly simple changes require a deep understanding of the entire codebase and its intricacies.

Not only must developers be careful not to inadvertently break existing functionality, but they must also identify the optimal location to implement changes without introducing potential issues down the line.

What if there was a way to consolidate all access control logic in a single centralized location?

Single Source of Truth

Here comes the ZenStack: a Typescirpt toolkit builds on top of Prisma. It uses the declarative data model on top of Prisma, adding access policy and validation rules, from which it will automatically generate RESTful or tRPC API for you.

SaaS project using ZenStack

Here is the SaaS backend project you can start with:

github.com/zenstackhq/saas-backend-temp…

Features

  • Multi-tenant
  • Soft delete
  • Sharing by group

Data Model

In schema.zmodel, there are 4 models, and their relationships are as below:

  • Organization is the top-level tenant. Any instance of User, post, and group belong to an organization.
  • One user could belong to multiple organizations and groups
  • One post belongs to a user and could belong to multiple groups.

Permissions

Let’s take a look at all the permissions of the Post and how they could be expressed using ZenStack’s access policies.

💡 You can find the detailed reference of access policies syntax below:
https://zenstack.dev/docs/reference/zmodel-language#access-policy

the owner must be set to the current user, and the organization must be set to one that the current user belongs to.

@@allow('create', owner == auth() && org.members?[this == auth()])

  • Update

    only the owner can update it and is not allowed to change the organization or owner

    @@allow('update', owner == auth() && org.future().members?[this == auth()] && future().owner == owner)

  • Read

    • allow the owner to read

      @@allow('read', owner == auth())

    • allow the member of the organization to read it if it’s public

      @@allow('read', isPublic && org.members?[this == auth()])

    • allow the group members to read it

      @@allow('read', groups?[users?[id == auth().id]])

  • Delete

    • don’t allow delete The operation is not allowed by default if no rule is specified for it.

    • The record is treated as deleted if isDeleted is true, aka soft delete.

      @@deny('all', isDeleted == true)

You can see the complete data model together with the above access policies defined in the

abstract model organizationBaseEntity { id String @id @default(uuid()) createdAt DateTime @default(now()) updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt isDeleted Boolean @default(false) @omit isPublic Boolean @default(false) owner User @relation(fields: [ownerId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade) ownerId String org Organization @relation(fields: [orgId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade) orgId String groups Group[] // when create, owner must be set to current user, and user must be in the organization @@allow('create', owner == auth() && org.members?[this == auth()]) // only the owner can update it and is not allowed to change the owner @@allow('update', owner == auth() && org.members?[this == auth()] && future().owner == owner) // allow owner to read @@allow('read', owner == auth()) // allow shared group members to read it @@allow('read', groups?[users?[this == auth()]]) // allow organization to access if public @@allow('read', isPublic && org.members?[this == auth()]) // can not be read if deleted @@deny('all', isDeleted == true)
} model Post extends organizationBaseEntity { title String content String
}

Model Inheritance

You may be curious about why these rules are defined within the abstract organizationBaseEntity model rather than the specific Post model. That’s why I say it is Scalable. With ZenStack's model inheritance capability, all common permissions can be conveniently handled within the abstract base model.

Consider the scenario where a newly hired developer needs to add a new ToDo model. He can effortlessly achieve this by simply extending the organizationBaseEntity :

model ToDo extends organizationBaseEntity { name String isCompleted Boolean @default(false)
}

All the multi-tenant, soft delete and sharing features will just work automatically. Additionally, if any specialized access control logic is required for ToDo, such as allowing shared individuals to update it, you can effortlessly add the corresponding policy rule within the ToDo model without concerns about breaking existing functionality:

@@allow('update', groups?[users?[this== auth()]] )

How much Typescript/JavaScript code do I need to write

So you already have the schema as the single source of the truth for your business model and your access control. You probably expect to see the Typescript/Javascript code you need to write next. Here is the exciting part:

That’s all. You'll hardly need to write any TS/JS code. The access control will be seamlessly handled behind the scenes by the code generated from the access policies we discussed earlier.

While you may observe several lines of code in index.ts, it is primarily for installing the automatically generated RESTful APIs into Express.js (which seamlessly integrates with all major Node.js frameworks) and starting the application.

Let’s play around

I created sample data for you to play around with. You can run the below command to seed it:

The data is like below:

So in the Prisma team, each user created a post:

  • Join Discord is not shared, so it could only be seen by Robin
  • Join Slack is shared in the group to which Robin belongs so that it can be seen by both Robin and Bryan.
  • Follow Twitter is a public one so that it could be seen by Robin, Bryan, and Gavin

You could simply call the Post endpoint to see the result simulate different users:

curl -H "X-USER-ID: robin@prisma.io" localhost:3000/api/post

💡 It uses the plain text of the user id just for test convenience. In the real world, you should use a more secure way to pass IDs like JWT tokens.

Based on the sample data, each user should see a different count of posts from 0 to 3.

Soft Delete

Since it’s soft delete, the actual operation is to update isDeleted to true. Let’s delete the “Join Salck” post of Robin by running below:

curl -X PUT \
-H "X-USER-ID: robin@prisma.io" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-d '{"data":{ "type":"post", "attributes":{ "isDeleted": true } } }'\
localhost:3000/api/post/slack

After that, if you try to access the Post endpoint again, the result won’t contain the “Join Slack” post anymore. If you are interested in how it works under the hook, check out another post for it:

Last

The SaaS product MermaidChart has recently launched its Teams feature powered by ZenStack. If you want to know about their experience of adopting ZenStack, you are welcome to join our Discord. For other information and examples, check out our official website.
item.author: jiasheng

item.title: GraphQL IDE：GraphiQL 与 Altair | GraphQL IDEs: GraphiQL vs Altair
item.description:
在 Web 开发领域，GraphQL 彻底改变了我们对 API 的看法。 与传统的 RESTful API 相比，GraphQL 使开发人员能够以更加灵活和高效的方式查询数据。 然而，与任何技术一样，您需要合适的工具来充分利用它。 今天，我们将比较两个 GraphQL 集成开发环境 (IDE)：GraphiQL 和 Altair。

单击下图观看此博文的YouTube 视频版本：

在深入比较之前，让我们快速了解一下 GraphQL IDE 是什么。 这些 IDE 提供多种功能，例如自动完成、错误突出显示和交互式文档。 它们帮助开发人员构建和测试 GraphQL 查询和变更，可视化返回的数据，并理解 GraphQL 架构的结构。

两个流行的 GraphQL IDE 是 GraphiQL 和 Altair。 让我们仔细看看每一个。

图形QL

它最初由 Facebook 开发，是一种浏览器内工具，使开发人员能够编写、验证和测试 GraphQL 查询。 它是开源的，可以集成到任何使用 GraphQL 的项目中。 最近，GraphiQL 进行了改进，具有新的 UI 和几个新功能，您可以在我之前写的博客文章中阅读。

模式自省： GraphiQL 提供了一个强大的模式视图，让您可以检查类型、字段和整体结构。

  • 自动补全：基于模式，GraphiQL 提供了自动补全功能，可以更轻松地构建复杂的查询。

  • 语法高亮显示：此功能可以更轻松地理解和浏览 GraphQL 查询和变更。 这包括错误。 如果你犯了语法错误，GraphiQL 会立即给它加下划线。

  • 交互式文档： GraphiQL 即时构建交互式 GraphQL API 文档，使 API 易于理解。

  • 查询历史：此功能使您可以访问以前的查询，因此您不必重写它们。

    • 如前所述，如果您想了解有关 GraphiQL 的更多信息，请查看我之前写的这篇博文。

    牵牛星

    它是开源的，可作为所有主要操作系统的桌面应用程序，以及 Chrome 和 Firefox 的网络扩展。

    多个窗口： Altair 允许您同时打开多个窗口，使您能够同时处理不同的查询（和 GraphQL API）。

  • 自动完成和错误突出显示：与 GraphiQL 一样，Altair 也提供自动完成和错误突出显示。

  • 订阅： Altair 支持 GraphQL 订阅，支持实时更新。

  • 文件上传： Altair 支持 GraphQL 多部分请求规范（Multipart request），允许您测试文件上传。

  • 预请求脚本：您可以编写在发送请求之前运行的脚本。 这对于处理复杂的身份验证流程很有用。

  • 集合：也许 Altair 最强大的功能是能够创建查询和变更的集合。 这允许您以逻辑方式跨多个 GraphQL API 组织查询和变更。

    • 当然，Altair 提供的功能还有很多。 如果您想了解有关 Altair 的更多信息，请查看官方文档。

    那么 GraphiQL 和 Altair 相比如何呢？ 让我们找出来。

    比较 GraphiQL 和 Altair

    但是，它们也有一些与众不同的属性。

    虽然 GraphiQL 的交互式文档和查询历史使 API 探索和重复查询更加舒适，但它缺少一些高级功能，如 Altair 提供的预请求脚本、文件上传和订阅。

    另一方面，Altair 凭借其广泛的功能集而大放异彩，提供的高级功能使其在复杂的用例中更加通用。 处理文件上传和同时使用多个 GraphQL API 的能力是一个巨大的优势。

    要查看 GraphiQL 和 Altair 的并排比较，请查看我的YouTube 频道上的视频。

    或者在 Twitter 上通过@gethackteam找到我。 我很想听听你的意见！

    这篇文章最初发表在hackteam.io上。 使用Reposted.io自动重新发布。



    原文:

    In the world of web development, GraphQL has revolutionized how we think about APIs. GraphQL enables developers to query data in a much more flexible and efficient way compared to traditional RESTful APIs. However, as with any technology, you need the right tools to make the most of it. Today, we will compare two GraphQL Integrated Development Environments (IDEs): GraphiQL and Altair.

    Click the image below to watch the YouTube video version of this blog post:

    Introduction to GraphQL IDEs

    Before diving into our comparison, let's quickly understand what a GraphQL IDE is. A GraphQL IDE is a tool that helps developers interact with GraphQL APIs, very similar to how your text-editor (like VSCode or IntelliJ) helps you write code.

    These IDEs provide several functionalities, such as auto-completion, error highlighting, and interactive documentation. They help developers to construct and test GraphQL queries and mutations, visualize the returned data, and understand the structure of the GraphQL schema.

    Two popular GraphQL IDEs are GraphiQL and Altair. Let's take a closer look at each.

    GraphiQL

    GraphiQL is one of the most well-known GraphQL IDEs. Originally developed by Facebook, it is an in-browser tool that enables developers to write, validate, and test GraphQL queries. It is open-source and can be integrated into any project that uses GraphQL. Recently, GraphiQL has been revamped with a new UI and several new features as you can read in ths blog post I wrote earlier.

    The most important features of GraphiQL are:

    • Schema introspection: GraphiQL offers a robust view of the schema, letting you examine types, fields, and overall structure.

    • Autocompletion: Based on the schema, GraphiQL provides autocompletion, making it easier to construct complex queries.

    • Syntax highlighting: This feature makes it easier to understand and navigate through the GraphQL queries and mutations. This includes errors. If you make a syntax mistake, GraphiQL will underline it immediately.

    • Interactive Documentation: GraphiQL builds an interactive GraphQL API documentation on-the-fly, making it easy to understand the API.

    • Query history: This feature lets you access your previous queries, so you don't have to rewrite them.

    As mentioned, if you want to learn more about GraphiQL then check out this blog post I wrote earlier.

    Altair

    Altair GraphQL Client is another impressive GraphQL IDE. It is open-source and available as a desktop app for all major operating systems, as well as a web extension for Chrome and Firefox.

    There's a lot of features that Altair offers that you can also find in GraphiQL, but here are some of the most important ones:

    • Multiple Windows: Altair allows you to open multiple windows at the same time, enabling you to work with different queries (and GraphQL APIs) simultaneously.

    • Autocompletion & Error Highlighting: Like GraphiQL, Altair also offers autocompletion and error highlighting.

    • Subscriptions: Altair supports GraphQL subscriptions, enabling real-time updates.

    • File Uploads: Altair supports GraphQL multipart request specification (Multipart request), allowing you to test file uploads.

    • Pre-request scripting: You can write scripts that run before the request is sent. This is useful for handling complex authentication flows.

    • Collections:: Perhaps the most powerful feature of Altair is the ability to create collections of queries and mutations. This allows you to organize your queries and mutations across multiple GraphQL APIs in a logical way.

    Of course, there are many more features that Altair offers. If you want to learn more about Altair, then check out the official documentation.

    So how do GraphiQL and Altair compare? Let's find out.

    Comparing GraphiQL and Altair

    GraphiQL and Altair share a common set of features like schema introspection, syntax highlighting, error highlighting, and auto-completion. However, they have some distinctive attributes as well.

    While GraphiQL's interactive documentation and query history make API exploration and reiteration of queries more comfortable, it lacks some advanced features like pre-request scripting, file uploads, and subscriptions, which are provided by Altair.

    On the other hand, Altair shines with its extensive feature set, providing advanced functionalities that make it more versatile for complex use-cases. The ability to handle file uploads and work with multiple GraphQL APIs simultaneously is a huge plus.

    To see a side-by-side comparison of GraphiQL and Altair, check out the video on my YouTube channel.

    Or find me on Twitter at @gethackteam. I'd love to hear from you!

    This post was originally published on hackteam.io. Reposted automatically with Reposted.io.
item.author: gethackteam

item.title: 打开开源之门：首次贡献者的简单步骤 | Opening Doors to Open Source: Easy Steps for First-Time Contributors
item.description:
我角色的成功与社区的成功成正比，也就是说，他们可以为 Amplication 的开源项目做出多大的成功，或者社区在欢迎新社区成员、帮助他们以及共同努力方面的繁荣程度如何作为一个社区，通过有价值来提升其他社区成员的技能。 这种方式的第一步是让我们的项目对想要贡献的新社区成员尽可能具有包容性。 这就是Issues for First Timers运动诞生的地方。

初学者的阻滞剂

当我开始考虑如何使我们的存储库具有包容性和欢迎初学者时，我想，为什么不考虑一下我早期作为贡献者的日子，当时我试图为许多新存储库做贡献并且每两个存储库都很少出现问题：

铺天盖地的代码

每当我想为一个新项目做贡献时，我都会去那个项目的 GitHub 存储库。 当我看到项目的代码库时，我发现很难理解那里发生了什么。 这总是让我觉得我不够好，无法做出贡献或必须学习更多。

这就是每个试图为新项目做出贡献的贡献者的感受。 这个问题的解决方案是有足够好的第一个问题，对初学者来说相对容易，让他们有机会了解更多关于包、技术、最佳实践等的信息。

缺乏支持

在我最初为开源项目做贡献的日子里，我不记得有多少次我不得不停止处理一个问题只是因为我需要更多的支持或项目维护者/支持的回应。

感觉不被欣赏

作为第一次贡献者，当我花费（大量）时间处理一个问题并为项目做出贡献时，我什至没有得到项目维护者的任何赞赏，我感到难过的是我的贡献看似还不够好。

当我们设计我们的旗舰项目“新手问题”时，我们的目标是解决所有这三个问题。 该程序的主要特点是：

好的第一期

我们专门为此次活动创建了一份良好的第一期清单，向您介绍了 Amplication 的代码库。 这些问题还包括设计和其他非代码问题。 我们的团队创建了所有问题，考虑到它们应该适合首次贡献者。 因此，此活动是您为开源做出贡献的最佳机会。

优先支持

我们为所有参与此活动的贡献者提供 Amplication 才华横溢的工程团队的优先支持，这样如果有任何问题，您并不孤单。

首次公关奖励

我们的设计团队总是做得很好，为 T 恤、帽子、贴纸、包等设计出很酷的赃物。您的努力会受到关注，并将获得限量版 Amplication 赃物作为奖励。

宣布最大版本

过去，我们已经通过我们的活动成功地为开源世界招募了 100 多名贡献者，例如 Ten Issues for First Timers、Dozen Issues for First Timers 等等。

但是，我们从未开展过如此大规模的活动； 这次我们有30 位贡献者准备为 Amplication 做出他们的第一个贡献。 我们的工程团队随时准备为您提供首次 PR 所需的所有支持，是的，限量版 Amplication T 恤正等着您合并 PR。 这一次为了让事情变得有趣，我们为社区设计了一个惊喜，这将在活动结束后揭晓😉。

包起来

虽然我们当前所有的问题都分配给了新的贡献者，但新的问题可能会出现，或者开发人员可能会放弃。 无论哪种情况，要符合资格，您都必须成为 Amplication 的新贡献者。 本质上，您之前不可能对 Amplication 做出过贡献。

您可以在此处查看此活动中可用的更多规则和所有问题： amplication/amplication#6270 。

记得加入我们的 discord寻求任何帮助。 快乐黑客！



原文:

"Of the community, By the community, For the community" were my words in the interview when I joined Amplication in November 2021 as a Community Manager. My role's success is directly proportional to the success of the community, and that is, how successfully they can contribute to the open source project of Amplication or how thriving the community together is in terms of welcoming the new community members, helping them, and together as a community to level up skills of other community members by being valuable. The first step in this way was to make our project as inclusive as possible for the new community members who wanted to contribute. That's where the Issues for First Timers campaign was born.

Blockers for First Timers

When I started thinking about how to make our repository inclusive and welcoming for first-timers, I thought, why not think about my early days as a contributor when I was trying to contribute to many new repositories and had few problems with every second repository:

Overwhelming Code

Whenever I want to contribute to a new project, I go to the GitHub repository of that project. When I see the project's codebase, I find it difficult to understand what is happening there. And this always gives me the sense that I am not good enough to contribute or have to learn more.

And this is what every contributor who tries to contribute to a new project feels. The solution for this problem is to have enough good first issues that are relatively easy for first-timers, giving them a chance to learn more about the packages, technologies, best practices, etc.

Lack of Support

During my initial days contributing to open-source projects, I can't remember how often I've had to stop working on an issue just because I needed more support or a response from the project maintainers/support.

Not Feeling Appreciated

As a first-time contributor, when I spend (a lot of) my time working on an issue and contributing something to the project, and I don't even get any appreciation from the project maintainers, I feel sad that my contributions are seemingly not good enough.

Amplication's Solution (Issues for First Timers)

When we designed our flagship program, "Issues for First Timers," we aimed to solve all three things. The main features of this program are:

Good First Issues

We create a list exclusively of good first issues for this campaign, which gives you an introduction to the Amplication's codebase. These issues include design and other non-code issues as well. Our team created all issues, considering they should be suitable for first-time contributors. So this campaign is your best chance to contribute to open source.

Priority Support

We provide priority support from the talented engineering team of Amplication to all the contributors participating in this campaign so that if there is any problem, you are not alone in it.

Rewards for Your First PR

Our design team always does a beautiful job coming up with cool swag designs for t-shirts, caps, sticker sheets, bags, etc. Your efforts are noticed and will be rewarded with limited edition Amplication swag.

Announcing the Biggest Edition

In the past, we have successfully onboarded 100+ contributors to the world of open source through our campaigns like Ten Issues for First Timers, Dozen Issues for First Timers, and many more.

However we've never ran a campaign as big as this; this time we have 30 contributors getting ready to make their first contribution to Amplication. Our Engineering team is more than prepared to provide you with all the support you need to make your first PR, and yes, a limited edition Amplication t-shirt is waiting for you to get your PR merged. This time to make things fun, we have made the design a surprise for the community, which will be revealed after the campaign ends 😉.

Wrapping Up

While all of our current issues are currently assigned to new contributors, new issues may come up, or developers may drop. To be eligible in either case you'll have to be a new contributor to Amplication. Essentially you can't have contributed to Amplication before.

You can check more rules and all the issues available in this campaign here: amplication/amplication#6270.

Remember to join our discord for any help. Happy Hacking!
item.author: sauain

item.title: Golang 中的插件 | Plugin in Golang
item.description:
这个存储库探索了 Go 1.8 中引入的插件特性，提供了一个示例来帮助您理解和利用这个强大的功能。

关于 Golang 中的插件特性

从 Go 1.8 开始，开发者可以编写插件并在运行时动态加载它们。 此功能为 Go 程序增加了新的灵活性，允许模块化和可扩展的设计。 使用插件，您可以扩展应用程序的功能，而无需重新编译或重新部署整个代码库。

主要好处

  • 动态加载：插件可以在运行时动态加载和卸载，可以在不中断主程序的情况下添加或删除功能。
  • 灵活性：与在构建时解析的传统库导入不同，插件提供了更灵活的方法。 它们可以独立交换或更新，让您可以自由地迭代和试验不同的实现。
  • 模块化：插件功能通过将特定功能封装在单独的组件中来促进模块化设计模式。 这增强了代码组织、可重用性和可维护性。
  • 封装：插件运行在自己独立的命名空间中，防止不同插件或主程序之间的冲突。 这允许安全可靠的可扩展性。

如何运行这个例子：

 go build -buildmode=plugin -o ./plugins/eng/eng.so ./plugins/eng/speaker.go

您可以使用 Makefile 快速构建所有插件：

 go run main.go english # Alice says "hello" in English go run main.go vietnamese # Anh Thư says "xin chào" in Vietnamese

如何实现插件

  • 插件包必须标识为 main。
  • 导出的包函数和变量成为共享库符号。 在上面，变量 Speaker 将在编译的共享库中作为符号导出。

示例实现：

 package main type speaker struct { } func (s *speaker) Speak() string { return "hello" } // Exported var Speaker speaker var SpeakerName = "Alice"

在此示例中，我们同时导出Speaker类型和SpeakerName变量，它们在插件上下文中称为symbols 。 通过导出这些符号，我们可以使它们对导入它们的其他包和模块具有可访问性和可见性。

如何打开插件

plugin, err := plugin.Open("path/to/plugin.so") if err != nil { return err }

如何查找符号

您可以通过以下语法在插件实现中查找已导出的符号：

 symSpeaker, err := plugin.Lookup("Speaker") if err != nil { return err } var speaker Speaker speaker, ok := symSpeaker.(Speaker) if !ok { return errors.New("unexpected type from module symbol") } speaker.Speak()

示例实现

一旦暂停，jacktt 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，jacktt 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，jacktt 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 jacktt 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 jacktt 的操作：

降低 jacktt 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging jacktt will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagning jacktt 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

 

This repository explores the plugin feature introduced in Go 1.8, providing a example to help you understand and utilize this powerful functionality.

About plugin feature in Golang

Starting from Go 1.8, developers have the ability to write plugins and load them dynamically at runtime. This feature adds a new level of flexibility to Go programs, allowing for modular and extensible designs. With plugins, you can extend your application's functionality without the need to recompile or redeploy the entire codebase.

Key benefit

  • Dynamic Loading: Plugins can be loaded and unloaded dynamically at runtime, enabling the addition or removal of functionality without disrupting the main program.
  • Flexibility: Unlike traditional library imports, which are resolved at build time, plugins offer a more flexible approach. They can be swapped or updated independently, giving you the freedom to iterate and experiment with different implementations.
  • Modularity: The plugin feature promotes a modular design pattern by encapsulating specific functionalities in separate components. This enhances code organization, reusability, and maintainability.
  • Encapsulation: Plugins run in their own isolated namespaces, preventing conflicts between different plugins or the main program. This allows for secure and reliable extensibility.

How to run this example: 

 go build -buildmode=plugin -o ./plugins/eng/eng.so ./plugins/eng/speaker.go

You can use the Makefile to quickly build all plugins:

go run main.go english # Alice says "hello" in English go run main.go vietnamese # Anh Thư says "xin chào" in Vietnamese

How to implement a plugin

  • A plugin package must be identified as main.
  • Exported package functions and variables become shared library symbols. In the above, variable Speaker will be exported as a symbol in the compiled shared library.

Example implementation:

package main type speaker struct {
} func (s *speaker) Speak() string { return "hello"
} // Exported
var Speaker speaker
var SpeakerName = "Alice"

In this example, we are exporting both the Speaker type and the SpeakerName variable, which are referred to as symbols in the context of plugins. By exporting these symbols, we enable their accessibility and visibility to other packages and modules that import them.

How to open plugin 

plugin, err := plugin.Open("path/to/plugin.so")
if err != nil { return err
}

How to look up symbol

You can look up the symbol that has been exported in plugin implementation by the following syntax:

symSpeaker, err := plugin.Lookup("Speaker")
if err != nil { return err
} var speaker Speaker
speaker, ok := symSpeaker.(Speaker)
if !ok { return errors.New("unexpected type from module symbol")
}
speaker.Speak()

Example implementation

github.com/huantt/golang-plugin-examle

Douglas Makey Mendez Molero - Jan 30

Once suspended, jacktt will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, jacktt will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by jacktt will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If jacktt is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag jacktt:

Make all posts by jacktt less visible

jacktt consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging jacktt will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: jacktt

item.title: 开源 101：入门指南 | Open Source 101: A Beginner's Guide to Getting Started
item.description:
开源的故事是关于社区的故事。 这是关于人们聚在一起，互相学习，共同成长，创造，发挥他们的才能和观点来创造伟大的东西。 这是一个关于当我们一起工作时人类可以做些什么的故事。 我不知道 Linus Torvalds 在 1990 年代初期创建 Linux 操作系统并免费向公众发布时是怎么想的，但我敢肯定这是关于以不同的方式做事。

当时，大多数操作系统都是专有软件，用户必须为其付费。 它是开源软件，这意味着源代码可供任何人免费使用、修改和分发。 开源的故事是创新、协作和社区之一。 这是一个每天都邀请你和我参与其中的故事。

在本初学者指南中，我们将探索开源开发的基础知识，包括项目选择和有意义的贡献——我们如何成为故事的一部分。 如果您准备好开始您的开源之旅或在开源中分享您的工作，请注册OpenSauced.pizza ！

为开源做贡献的好处

为开源做贡献我最喜欢的好处是结识新朋友并成为新社区的一部分。 由于这些关系，我获得的长期利益是令人难以置信的回报。 但这并不是贡献的唯一原因。 这里还有几个原因：

  • 学习新技能：开源项目是学习新编程技能的好方法。 您可以从其他开发人员的代码中学习，也可以从社区中获得对您自己的代码的反馈。
  • 有所作为：开源软件被全世界数百万人使用。 通过为开源项目做贡献，您可以帮助制作该软件。
  • 获得实际工作经验：当您为开源项目做出贡献时，您将展示您在多个领域的技能，包括：
  • 编程：编写既实用又可读的代码。
  • 解决问题：识别和解决代码中的问题。
  • 协作：与其他开发人员合作以实现共同目标。
  • 沟通：无论是在撰写文档还是讨论您正在处理的问题时，清晰简洁地传达您的想法。

您可以在Beyond Content Creation 中查看更多原因：开源贡献如何帮助您获得关注。

开源入门

当您尝试开始时，有很多事情需要考虑。 如果您在理解术语方面需要帮助，请从这篇文章开始： 开源术语 101：新贡献者入门。 熟悉术语后，您需要找到合适的项目。 查看 选择你自己的开源冒险：成功之路探索参与开源项目的不同原因，以帮助你弄清楚从哪里开始。 开始时的一些其他注意事项包括：
找到一个活跃的项目。 如果存储库有一段时间没有更新，并且维护人员没有对问题、讨论或评论做出响应，则表明该项目不活跃。

如果你查看他们的存储库，你应该能够看到最后一个 PR 是何时合并的。

  • 与为开源做出贡献的人交谈。 对新项目最好的介绍通常是个人介绍。 询问您所属的技术社区中的人，或访问OpenSauced Insights ，并查看您感兴趣的技术的贡献者列表，并与他们联系以了解更多关于他们为他们的项目做出贡献的经验是的一部分。
  • 找一个有初学者友好问题的项目。 带有“良好的第一期”或“初学者友好”标签的项目通常对初学者有好处。

如果没有适合初学者的标签，请不要忘记最好的第一期是您自己写的那期。

  • 阅读项目文档。 找到想要参与的项目后，阅读项目文档非常重要。 这将帮助您了解项目的目标、代码库和编码标准。 始终通读 README 和 CONTRIBUTING 指南。 您不仅可以更好地了解项目，而且应该解释和明确对如何贡献的期望。

查看OpenSauced 社区健康档案。

  • 与社区互动。 加入项目的沟通渠道，如 Slack 或Discord 、他们的讨论、他们的博客，并自我介绍。 提出问题并表达您对贡献的兴趣。 成为社区的一员将扩大您的联系，帮助您深入了解正在进行的讨论和开发时间表，并且可能是获得经验丰富的贡献者指导的机会。
  • 从小做起。 寻找小且易于修复的问题。 随着您获得经验，您可以开始处理更大的问题或承担更多的责任。 如果您遇到了无法完成的问题，请不要害怕寻求帮助或让维护人员知道。 这都是过程的一部分。
  • 要有耐心。 学习如何为开源项目做贡献需要时间。 如果您第一次没有把所有事情都做好，不要气馁。 只要继续学习和练习，你最终就会掌握它的窍门。

社区协作

成为开源项目的一部分就是成为社区的一部分。 他们会在能力范围内支持和帮助您。 这里有一些提示，可帮助您充分利用社区体验。

  • 查看行为准则 (COC)——或者如果他们没有，请创建一个问题要求一个。 请求帮忙。 如果您遇到困难，不确定该怎么做或如何沟通，请提出问题。 具体说明您面临的问题以及您已采取的解决该问题的步骤。
  • 提供支持和协助。 无论您有多少经验，总有人比您落后一步，您可以提供帮助，并且认识到您可以通过提供支持和独特的见解在各个阶段帮助他人，这有助于加强社区并营造积极的环境用于学习和成长。 积极参与讨论，分享见解，为项目和社区做出贡献。
  • 代码审查是开源贡献过程的重要组成部分。 将代码审查视为成长和改进的机会，并尊重审查人员和项目维护人员提供的反馈和建议，即使他们挑战您的初始方法。 优雅地合并反馈，并努力理解提出的问题。

文档和技术写作

为文档做贡献是支持开源项目的一种有效方式。 执行此操作的一些方法包括：

  • 确定可以改进文档的区域，例如缺失或过时的信息、不明确的说明或不完整的解释。 改进文档使新贡献者更容易理解项目并为项目做出贡献，从而营造一个更具包容性和欢迎性的环境。
  • 清楚简洁地记录您的更改。 解释任何代码修改，包括注意事项。 清晰的文档有助于未来的开发人员有效地理解和维护代码库。
  • 创建指南或教程是另一种贡献方式。 通过创建或改进用户指南、教程或其他教育材料来分享您的知识和专长。 拥有资源有助于有效地吸引新用户并使他们能够充分利用项目的功能。

贡献超越代码

您不必知道如何编写代码即可为开源做出贡献。 以下是其他一些贡献方式：

  • 项目管理：组织问题、创建项目路线图或协调发布。
  • 翻译：许多项目需要翻译才能让更广泛的受众访问该项目。
  • 分类：帮助进行错误分类或参与论坛支持社区。 您可以解决用户报告的问题、重现错误或提供帮助。 这提高了项目的稳定性和用户体验。
  • 推广：积极分享项目有助于提高知名度并吸引更多的贡献者和用户。 分享您对该项目的积极体验、撰写有关该项目的博客文章或文章，或者在会议或聚会上就该项目发表演讲，有助于项目的发展和影响。

应对挑战和学习机会

冒名顶替综合症和自我怀疑会影响任何人，尤其是当您刚刚起步时。 感到不确定是正常的。 每个人的旅程都会有所不同。 重要的是要让支持你的社区成员围绕在你身边，寻求鼓励，并记住这是一个学习之旅——会有成长的烦恼。

此旅程的一部分意味着您将收到反馈和建设性的批评。 这些都是成长的机会。 倾听、理解他人的观点，并利用反馈来成长为贡献者。

将协作视为向他人学习、分享想法和建立有意义的关系的机会。

学习贡献的基础知识

随着您的进步，在您开始开源之旅时学习基础知识很重要。 这里有一些需要理解的关键概念。

版本控制系统

版本控制系统 (VCS) 是帮助开发人员在协作开发环境中管理对源代码和其他文件的更改的工具。 这意味着多人可以同时在同一个代码库上工作，同时跟踪更改、促进协作并提供随时间进行的修改历史记录。 您可以查看Git 的官方文档以获取更多信息。

编码技巧

每个项目都会有自己的策略来编写高效、可维护和高质量的代码。 其中一些考虑因素包括：

  • 代码组织：代码的结构将影响可读性和可维护性。 要了解有关如何做出这些决策的更多信息，请查看模块化、关注点分离和设计模式等概念。
  • 代码可读性：编写易于理解的代码有助于协作并减少出现错误的可能性。 做到这一点的方法包括使用描述性变量名称、编写清晰的注释和一致的格式。
  • 性能优化：编写高效的代码可以提高速度和资源使用率。 提高性能的方法包括：算法优化、数据结构选择、缓存和分析。
  • 错误处理：当我们发布软件时，重要的是要考虑如何处理错误。 发生错误时会发生什么？
  • 代码测试：每个项目都会有不同的方法并使用不同的软件来编写测试。 他们可能会实施单元测试、集成测试和测试驱动开发 (TDD) 或其他方法或方法组合，以确保他们交付可靠的代码。

项目贡献工作流程

因为开源项目有维护者和贡献者协作创建，所以有一些流程有助于确保积极的合作体验、确保代码质量和维护健康的代码库，这一点很重要。 作为其中的一部分，实施了一些工作流程，包括：

  • 代码版本控制和协作：我们在上面讨论了版本控制系统。 了解如何克隆存储库、创建分支、提交更改和推送代码以及了解分叉、分支和拉取请求等协作工作流非常重要。 我强烈建议您创建自己的练习项目，更好的是，请一位刚接触编码的朋友一起练习！ 有关如何执行此操作的更多信息，请查看我们关于如何提交贡献的开源介绍部分
  • 问题跟踪：问题用于报告错误、建议功能或讨论改进。 了解您正在处理的项目如何使用问题、标签和优先级。 您可以在我们的Insights 存储库中查看我们如何处理问题。
  • 沟通：花一些时间探索项目如何沟通。 他们是否使用讨论板或异步通信平台（如 Slack 或 Discord）？
  • 贡献：在贡献之前，查看项目的指南和标准。 准备好根据反馈更新您的合并请求 (PR) 并与其他贡献者协作。 提交 PR 后会发生很多事情。 如此之多，以至于我写了“向开源项目提交 PR 后会发生什么？” 不接受 PR 的原因可能有很多。 你可以在这里找到更多相关信息。

首次贡献者资源

有关入门的更多资源，请查看以下内容：

为开源做贡献是学习新技能、与其他贡献者联系并产生积极影响的机会。 请记住，维护者是努力工作以使项目继续进行的人。 始终保持尊重、耐心和友善。 希望本指南对您的开源之旅有所帮助。 如果您有任何问题，请告诉我们！ 我们期待很快在 OpenSauced 上看到您的亮点。

使用 Javascript 编码工艺 - 6 月 8 日

一旦暂停，opensauced 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

解除暂停后，opensauced 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，opensauced 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 opensauced 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 opensauced：

降低 opensauced 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 opensauced 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

The story of open source is a story about community. It’s about people coming together, learning from each other, growing together, and creating, of bringing their talents and perspectives to build something great. It’s a story of what humanity can do when we work together. I don’t know what Linus Torvalds thought when he created the Linux operating system and released it to the public for free in the early 1990s, but I’m sure it was about doing things differently.

At the time, most operating systems were proprietary software, and users had to pay for them. Linux was different. It was open source software, which meant that the source code was freely available for anyone to use, modify, and distribute. The story of open source is one of innovation, collaboration, and community. And it’s a story that invites you and me into it every day.

In this beginner's guide, we'll explore the fundamentals of open source development, including project selection and meaningful contributions–how we can be part of the story. And if you’re ready to start on your open source journey or to share your work in open source, sign up for OpenSauced.pizza!

Benefits to Contributing to Open Source

My favorite benefit of contributing to Open Source is meeting new people and being a part of new communities. The long-term benefits I’ve had because of those relationships have been incredibly rewarding. But that’s not the only reason to contribute. Here are a few more reasons:

  • Learn new skills: Open source projects are a great way to learn new programming skills. You can learn from the code of other developers, and you can get feedback on your own code from the community.
  • Make a difference: Open source software is used by millions of people around the world. By contributing to open source projects, you can help make that software.
  • Gain real-work experience: When you contribute to an open source project, you are demonstrating your skills in a number of areas, including:
  • Programming: Writing code that is both functional and readable.
  • Problem-solving: Identifying and solving problems in code.
  • Collaboration: Working with other developers to achieve a common goal.
  • Communication: Communicating your ideas clearly and concisely, whether that’s in writing documentation or discussing the issue you’re working on.

You can check out more reasons in Beyond Content Creation: How Open Source Contributions Can Help You Get Noticed.

Getting Started with Open Source

There are a lot of things to consider when you’re trying to get started. If you need help understand the terminology, start with this post: Open Source Terminology 101: A Primer for New Contributors. Once you’re familiar with the terminology, you need to find the right project. Check out Choose Your Own Adventure in Open Source: Paths to Success to explore different reasons for getting involved in open source projects to help you figure out where to get started. Some other considerations when you’re getting started, include:
Find an active project. If a repository hasn’t been updated in a while and the maintainers aren’t responding to issues, discussions, or comments, that’s a sign that the project isn’t active.

If you look at their repository, you should be able to see when the last PR was merged in.

  • Talk to people who are contributing to open source. The best introduction to a new project is often a personal one. Ask people in tech communities you’re a part of, or go to OpenSauced Insights, and check out the list of contributors for the technology you’re interested in and reach out to them to learn more about their experiences contributing to the projects they’re a part of.
  • Find a project that has beginner-friendly issues. Projects with "good first issues" or "beginner-friendly" labels are often good for beginners.

If there aren’t any labels for beginners, don’t forget that the best first issue is the one that you write yourself.

  • Read the project documentation. Once you’ve found a project that you want to contribute to, it’s important to read the project documentation. This will help you understand the project's goals, its codebase, and its coding standards. Always read through the README and CONTRIBUTING guides. Not only can you get a better understanding of the project, but the expectations for how to contribute should be explained and clear.

Check out OpenSauced Community Health Files.

  • Engage with the Community. Join the project’s communication channels, like Slack or Discord, their discussions, their blog, and introduce yourself. Ask questions and express your interest in contributing. Becoming a part of the community will expand your connections, help you to gain insights into ongoing discussions and development timelines, and may be an opportunity for mentorship from experienced contributors.
  • Start small. Look for issues that are small and easy to fix. As you gain experience, you can start working on larger issues or taking on more responsibility. Don’t be afraid to ask for help or to let the maintainers know if you’ve taken on an issue that you can’t complete. It’s all part of the process.
  • Be patient. It takes time to learn how to contribute to an open source project. Don't get discouraged if you don't get everything right the first time. Just keep learning and practicing, and you'll eventually get the hang of it.

Community Collaboration

Being part of an open source project is being part of a community. They are there to support and help you within their capacity. Here are some tips to get the most out of the community experience.

  • Review the Code of Conduct (COC)–or if they don’t have one, create an issue asking for one. The COC is there to keep the community safe and supported and it’s essential to understand what that means in the context of your community.
  • Ask for help. If you’re stuck, not sure what to do or how to communicate, ask a question. Be specific about the issue you're facing and the steps you've already taken to address it.
  • Offer Support and Assistance. It doesn’t matter how much experience you have, there’s always someone a step behind you that you can help, and recognizing that you can help others at all stages by providing your support and unique insight can help strengthen the community and foster a positive environment for learning and growth. Actively participate in discussions, share insights, and contribute to the project and community.
  • Code reviews are an important part of the open source contribution process. Embrace code reviews as opportunities for growth and improvement, and respect the feedback and suggestions provided by reviewers and project maintainers, even if they challenge your initial approach. Incorporate the feedback gracefully, and strive to understand the concerns raised.

Documentation and Technical Writing

Contributing to documentation is an impactful way to support an open source project. Some ways to do this include:

  • Identify areas where documentation can be improved, like missing or outdated information, unclear instructions, or incomplete explanations. Improving the documentation makes it easier for new contributors to understand and contribute to the project, which leads to a more inclusive and welcoming environment.
  • Document your changes clearly and concisely. Explain any code modifications, including considerations. Clear documentation helps future developers understand and maintain the codebase effectively.
  • Creating guides or tutorials is another way to contribute. Share your knowledge and expertise by creating or improving user guides, tutorials, or other educational materials. Having resources helps to effectively onboard new users and empower them to make the most of the project's capabilities.

Contributing Beyond Code

You don’t have to know how to code to contribute to open source. Non-code contributions can have a huge impact. Here are some other ways to contribute:

  • Project management: organize issues, create project roadmaps, or coordinate releases.
  • Translations:Many projects need translations to make the project accessible to a wider audience.
  • Triaging: Helping with bug triaging or participating in forums supports the community. You can address user-reported issues, reproduce bugs, or offer help. This improves the project's stability and the user experience.
  • Promotion: Actively sharing the project helps raise awareness and attract more contributors and users. Sharing your positive experiences with the project, writing blog posts or articles about it, or speaking at conferences or meetups about it, helps its growth and impact.

Dealing with Challenges and Learning Opportunities

Imposter syndrome and self-doubt can affect anyone, especially when you’re starting out. It's normal to feel uncertain. Everyone’s journey will look different. It’s important to surround yourself with supportive community members, seek encouragement, and remember that this is a learning journey–there will be growing pains.

Part of this journey means that you’ll receive feedback and constructive criticism. These are opportunities to grow. Listen, understand the perspectives of others, and use feedback to grow as a contributor.

Embrace collaboration as an opportunity to learn from others, share ideas, and build meaningful relationships.

Learning the Basics of Contributing

As you progress, it’s important to learn the basics as you begin your open source journey. Here are some key concepts to understand.

Version Control System

Version control systems (VCS) are tools that help developers manage changes to source code and other files in a collaborative development environment. This means that multiple people can work on the same codebase simultaneously while keeping track of changes, facilitating collaboration, and providing a history of modifications made over time. Git is one of the most widely used VCS, allowing for capabilities like forking, cloning, and pulling. You can check out the official documentation for Git for more information.

Coding Techniques

Every project will have its own strategies for writing efficient, maintainable, and high-quality code. Some of those considerations include:

  • Code Organization: How the code is structured will impact readability and maintainability. To learn more about how these decisions are made, look into concepts like modularization, separation of concerns, and design patterns.
  • Code Readability: Writing code that’s easy to understand helps in collaboration and reduces the likelihood of bugs. Ways to do that include using descriptive variable names, writing clear comments, and consistent formatting.
  • Performance Optimization: Writing efficient code can improve the speed and resource usage. Ways to improve performancing include: algorithm optimizations, data structure choices, caching, and profiling.
  • Error Handling: When we ship software, it’s important to think about how to handle errors. What happens when an error occurs?
  • Code Testing: Each project will have different approaches and use different software for writing tests. They might implement unit testing, integration testing, and test-driven development (TDD) or other methods or combinations of methods to ensure they’re shipping reliable code.

Project Contribution Workflows

Because open source projects have maintainers and contributors collaborating to create, it’s important that there are processes that help to ensure a positive experience working together, ensure code quality, and maintain a healthy codebase. As part of this, there are some workflows implemented, including:

  • Code Versioning and Collaboration: We talked about this above with Version Control Systems. It’s important to learn how to clone repositories, create branches, commit changes, and push code as well as understand collaboration workflows like forking, branching, and pull requests. I highly recommend creating your own projects for practicing, and even better, bring in a friend who’s new to coding and practice together! For more information on how to do this, check out our Intro to Open Source section on How to submit a contribution
  • Issue Tracking: Issues are used to report bugs, suggest features, or discuss improvements. Understand how the project you’re working on uses issues, labeling, and prioritization. You can check out how we handle issues in our Insights repository.
  • Communication: Take some time to explore how the project communicates. Are they using discussion boards or async communication platforms like Slack or Discord?
  • Contributing: Before contributing, review the project's guidelines and standards. Be prepared to update your Pull Request (PR) based on feedback and work collaboratively with other contributors. There’s a lot that happens after your PR is submitted. So much so, that I wrote “What happens after you submit a PR to an Open Source Project?”. There can be a lot of reasons why a PR isn’t accepted. You can find out more about that here.

First Time Contributor Resources

For more resources on getting started, check out the following:

Contributing to open source is an opportunity to learn new skills, connect with other contributors, and to make a positive impact. Keep in mind that maintainers are human beings who work hard to keep projects going. Always be respectful, patient, and kind. Hopefully, this guide is helpful on your open source journey. If you have any questions, let us know! We look forward to seeing your highlight on OpenSauced soon.

Code Craft-Fun with Javascript - Jun 8

Once suspended, opensauced will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, opensauced will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by opensauced will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If opensauced is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag opensauced:

Make all posts by opensauced less visible

opensauced consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging opensauced will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: bekahhw

item.title: 让我们用 Tailwind 和 JavaScript 构建一个轮播吧！ | Let's build a carousel with Tailwind and JavaScript!
item.description:
在构建它之前，首先我们需要仔细考虑轮播是否是我们需要展示的最佳选择。 轮播通常不是您想要的解决方案：请查看此站点以获取一些相关统计数据。

如果您确定轮播最适合您的 UI，请继续阅读。

我将从这里解释 HTML 布局开始。 我正在使用 Tailwind，因为那是我当时使用的，但您可以使用任何您喜欢的 CSS。

首先，我们有一个开始标签：

 <div id="animation-carousel" class="tw-relative tw-w-full tw-h-36 md:tw-h-96 tw-my-8" data-carousel>

一些注意事项：我们将在这里使用大量的相对和绝对定位。 为了确保在这个容器范围内，我们确保位置是relative 。 因为每个项目都将以这种方式定位，所以我们也设置了一个特定的height 。

最后，我们正在设置data属性。 这就是我们要在 JavaScript 中获取的内容。 我可以很容易地使用一个类名。

接下来，我得到了内包装纸。 这将包含图像、上一个和下一个按钮以及指示器（您有时会看到的小导航点）。

 <div class="tw-relative tw-overflow-hidden tw-rounded-lg tw-h-36 md:tw-h-96">

指标

指标在此设置中是可选的。 如果它们不在 DOM 中，我们就不会渲染它们。 为方便起见，我使用了一个模板标签，因此它们最初不会呈现，相反，我们可以根据需要呈现点数多次克隆该模板的内部 HTML：
