item.title: 外包测试服务的 5 大好处 | 5 Benefits of Outsourcing Your Testing Services
item.description:
在当今竞争激烈的商业环境中，确保软件应用程序的质量和可靠性变得越来越重要。 强大的测试服务在识别错误​​、增强用户体验和确保无缝功能方面发挥着关键作用。

然而，管理内部测试团队可能既耗费资源又耗时。 这就是外包您的测试服务发挥作用的地方，它提供了许多优势。

在本文中，我们将探索外包测试服务领域，并揭示等待您的五个特殊优势。 从提高效率到优化成本，我们将揭示外包如何提升您的测试流程并推动您的业务走向成功。

清楚地掌握这些优势后，您可以自信地重塑您的测试程序并为胜利铺平道路。
获得专业知识和专业资源


在测试服务方面，外包为您提供了宝贵的专业知识和专业资源，可以将您的测试工作提升到新的高度。

通过与外包合作伙伴联手，利用测试领域经验丰富的专业人员的专业知识。 这些专家通过多年的实践磨练了他们的技能，并配备了最新的工具和技术来提供一流的结果。

使外包公司与众不同的是他们可以获得尖端的人工智能技术。 借助 AI，他们可以简化和自动化测试流程，从而显着提高效率和准确性。

利用 AI 功能，这些公司解锁了发现模式、预测问题和增强测试策略的能力。 人类专业知识与人工智能工具相结合，使外包公司能够提供卓越的测试服务，满足您的独特需求并推动您的业务向前发展。

成本优化和效率提高


外包测试服务可以彻底改变成本优化和效率，将您的测试流程提升到新的成功高度。

与值得信赖的测试服务提供商合作可以让您获得他们丰富的专业知识和资源，同时有效地减少您的财务承诺。 这消除了广泛的内部培训和招聘的需要，为您节省了时间和金钱。

此外，外包公司通常拥有由人工智能/机器学习支持的尖端测试工具和技术，可以提高测试程序的有效性和准确性。

当您将测试要求交到专业人员手中时，您将开启一个精简而细致的测试流程，从而节省您宝贵的时间和资源。

将成本优化和效率提高作为外包测试服务的支柱，您可以为您的组织释放更大的价值，同时在竞争激烈的市场中保持领先地位。

加快上市时间


通过在当今动态的商业环境中外包您的测试服务，加快您的上市时间并获得竞争优势。 与专业的测试服务提供商建立强大的联盟，组建一支技术精湛的专家团队，他们巧妙地指导您轻松、准确地完成测试之旅。

凭借简化的工作流程和先进的工具，这些专业人员可以迅速识别并解决任何问题，确保您的产品或服务经过全面测试并及时上市。

此外，外包测试服务可以释放您的内部资源，让您的团队能够专注于创新和产品开发等核心业务活动。

聘请外部专家执行测试任务可提高效率和生产力，并为您的组织维护一流的质量标准。

提高软件质量和客户满意度


通过外包您的测试服务来提高软件质量和客户满意度。 有测试专家在您身边，您的软件会经过细致的评估，在到达您的客户时不会留下任何缺陷或错误的余地。 这些专业人员进行全面的功能、性能和可用性测试，以确保无缝体验。

通过外包测试服务，您可以让内部资源专注于重要的业务领域，提高生产力并释放增长机会。 这使您能够交付超出客户期望的软件解决方案并培养长期忠诚度。

凭借改进的软件质量和满意的客户，您的企业将获得竞争优势，提高声誉，并培养推动增长的牢固关系。

当专家处理您的测试需求时，流程变得精简，节省时间和资源，软件质量飙升。 这最终会带来更快乐的客户和更成功的业务。

适应性和可扩展性


当您外包测试服务时，您将获得适应性和可扩展性的优势，以满足您不断变化的业务需求。 信誉良好的测试服务提供商可以根据您的项目要求灵活调整其资源和能力。

此外，外包提供了利用广泛的专业知识和技能组合的机会。 测试服务提供商通常拥有多元化的专家团队，他们精通各种测试方法、工具和技术。

这可确保您的测试流程根据您的特定需求量身定制，并由具备必要知识和经验的专业人员执行。

外包测试服务的适应性和可扩展性使您的企业能够驾驭动态市场条件并根据需要扩展您的测试工作。 这种适应性不仅可以提高效率，还可以让您始终处于竞争的前沿，确保为您的客户提供一流的软件解决方案。

结论

外包您的测试服务具有众多优势，可以显着影响您的业务成功。 通过与专业测试服务提供商合作，您可以获得丰富的专业知识、专业资源和尖端技术来优化测试。 这使您可以加快上市时间、降低成本并提高客户满意度。

外包您的测试服务使您的内部资源能够专注于重要的业务活动，推动指数增长和进步。 因此，利用外包带来的好处，并定位您的业务以提高绩效、创新和客户满意度。



原文:

In today's competitive business landscape, ensuring the quality and reliability of software applications has become increasingly crucial. Robust testing services play a pivotal role in identifying bugs, enhancing user experience, and ensuring seamless functionality.

However, managing an in-house testing team can be both resource-intensive and time-consuming. This is where outsourcing your testing services comes into play, offering numerous advantages.

In this article, we will explore the realm of outsourcing testing services and reveal the five exceptional advantages that await you. From enhanced efficiency to cost optimization, we will uncover how outsourcing can elevate your testing processes and drive your business toward success.

With a clear grasp of these advantages, you can confidently reshape your testing procedures and pave the way for triumph.
Access to Expertise and Specialized Resources


Image Source

When it comes to testing services, outsourcing provides you with invaluable access to expertise and specialized resources that can take your testing efforts to new heights.

Harness the expertise of seasoned professionals in the realm of testing by joining forces with an outsourcing partner. These experts have honed their skills through years of practice and are equipped with the latest tools and technologies to deliver top-notch results.

What sets outsourcing companies apart is their access to cutting-edge AI technologies. With AI at their disposal, they can streamline and automate testing processes, significantly enhancing efficiency and accuracy.

Leveraging AI capabilities, these companies unlock the ability to uncover patterns, predict issues, and enhance testing strategies. The combination of human expertise and AI-powered tools allows outsourcing companies to deliver exceptional testing services that meet your unique requirements and drive your business forward.

Cost Optimization and Increased Efficiency


Image Source

Outsourcing testing services can revolutionize cost optimization and efficiency, elevating your testing processes to new heights of success.

Collaborating with a trustworthy testing service provider grants you access to their wealth of expertise and resources, all while effectively minimizing your financial commitments. This eliminates the need for extensive in-house training and recruitment, saving you both time and money.

Furthermore, outsourcing firms typically possess cutting-edge testing tools and technologies empowered by AI/ML that can augment effectiveness and precision in testing procedures.

When you place your testing requirements in the hands of professionals, you unlock a streamlined and meticulous testing process that conserves your precious time and resources.

With cost optimization and increased efficiency as the pillars of outsourcing your testing services, you can unlock greater value for your organization while staying ahead in a competitive market.

Accelerated Time-to-Market


Image Source

Accelerate your time-to-market and gain a competitive edge by outsourcing your testing services in today's dynamic business landscape. Forge a powerful alliance with a specialized testing service provider, unlocking a skilled team of experts who deftly guide you through the testing journey with precision and ease.

With their streamlined workflows and advanced tools, these professionals can swiftly identify and resolve any issues, ensuring that your products or services are thoroughly tested and ready for market in a timely manner.

Furthermore, outsourcing testing services free up your internal resources, allowing your team to focus on core business activities such as innovation and product development.

Enlisting external experts for testing tasks boosts efficiency and productivity and upholds top-notch quality standards for your organization.

Improved Software Quality and Customer Satisfaction


Image Source

Elevate software quality and customer satisfaction by outsourcing your testing services. With testing experts by your side, your software undergoes meticulous evaluation, leaving no room for flaws or bugs when it reaches your customers. These professionals conduct comprehensive functional, performance, and usability testing to guarantee a seamless experience.

Through outsourcing testing services, you empower your internal resources to focus on vital business areas, driving productivity and unlocking growth opportunities. This allows you to deliver software solutions that exceed customer expectations and cultivate long-term loyalty.

With improved software quality and satisfied customers, your business gains a competitive edge, enhances its reputation, and fosters strong relationships that drive growth.

When experts handle your testing needs, the process becomes streamlined, time and resources are saved, and software quality soars. This ultimately leads to happier customers and a more successful business.

Adaptability and Scalability


Image Source

When you outsource your testing services, you gain the advantage of adaptability and scalability to meet the evolving needs of your business. A reputable testing service provider possesses the flexibility to adjust their resources and capabilities according to your project requirements.

Furthermore, outsourcing offers the opportunity to tap into a wide range of expertise and skillsets. Testing service providers often have a diverse team of specialists who are well-versed in various testing methodologies, tools, and technologies.

This ensures that your testing processes are tailored to your specific needs and conducted by professionals who possess the necessary knowledge and experience.

The adaptability and scalability of outsourcing testing services empower your business to navigate dynamic market conditions and scale your testing efforts as needed. This adaptability not only improves efficiency but also keeps you at the forefront of the competition, ensuring the delivery of top-notch software solutions to your clients.

Conclusion

Outsourcing your testing services offers numerous advantages that can significantly impact the success of your business. Through collaboration with a specialized testing service provider, you unlock a wealth of expertise, specialized resources, and cutting-edge technologies for optimized testing. This allows you to accelerate your time-to-market, reduce costs, and improve customer satisfaction.

Outsourcing your testing services empowers your internal resources to concentrate on vital business activities, propelling exponential growth and progress. So, take advantage of the benefits that outsourcing brings and position your business for enhanced performance, innovation, and customer satisfaction.
item.author: enis-azemi

item.title: bash 聊天GPT | bash ChatGPT
item.description:
彼得维沃

发表于 6 月 21 日

我经常需要使用Linux / GitBash终端快速向 ChatGPT 询问一些事情，所以我编写了这个 bash 脚本来实现这一点。 主要目标是终端友好输出，对 gitbash 没有任何额外的依赖要求，所以我使用了curl 、 sed和fold 。

写信给 chatgpt.sh - 不要忘记你的钥匙！

 #!/bin/sh curl=`cat <<EOS curl -sS --location --insecure --request POST 'https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions' \ --header 'Authorization: Bearer <OPEN_AI_KEY>' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data-raw '{ "model": "gpt-3.5-turbo", "max_tokens": 2000, "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "$1"}] }' EOS` eval ${curl} | sed -En 's/.*"content": "(.*)"/\1/p' | sed 's/\\n/\n/g' | fold -s

也许添加到~/.bashrc一些alias

 alias ai='sh ~/chathgpt.sh'

使用

ai "write bash chatgpt call which give formated text result"

编码愉快！

只是另一个 React Dev - 5 月 27 日

暂停后，pvivo 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到取消暂停为止。

一旦解除暂停，pvivo 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，pvivo 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 pvivo 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 pvivo 的方法：

降低 pvivo 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 pvivo 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Peter Vivo

Posted on Jun 21

 

I often need to quickly ask ChatGPT something using a Linux/GitBash terminal, so I wrote this bash script to facilitate that. Main goal is terminal friendly output, without any additional dependency requirement for gitbash, so I used curl,sed and fold.

write to chatgpt.sh - do not forget your key!

#!/bin/sh
curl=`cat <<EOS
curl -sS --location --insecure --request POST 'https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer <OPEN_AI_KEY>' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--data-raw '{ "model": "gpt-3.5-turbo", "max_tokens": 2000, "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "$1"}]
}'
EOS`
eval ${curl} | sed -En 's/.*"content": "(.*)"/\1/p' | sed 's/\\n/\n/g' | fold -s

maybe add to ~/.bashrc some alias

alias ai='sh ~/chathgpt.sh'

use 

ai "write bash chatgpt call which give formated text result"

Happy coding!

Just another React Dev - May 27

Once suspended, pvivo will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, pvivo will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by pvivo will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If pvivo is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag pvivo:

Make all posts by pvivo less visible

pvivo consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging pvivo will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: pvivo

item.title: 在 Kubernetes 集群中自动管理 AWS ECR 凭证 | Automatic Management of AWS ECR Credentials in a Kubernetes Cluster
item.description:
在使用AWS ECR (Elastic Container Registry)的过程中，我遇到了一个问题：存储库访问密钥每六小时过期一次。 使用非 AWS Kubernetes 测试集群时，我不得不不断地手动更新这些凭证，这是一个重复且乏味的过程。

从这次经历中产生了创建一个自动化这个过程的工具的想法： k8s-aws-ecr-secret-updater 。 此工具是一个 Kubernetes cronjob，旨在自动更新 AWS ECR 存储库访问凭证。

定时作业配置

用于创建 cronjob 的 YAML 代码由几个部分组成，我现在将逐个分析它们：

有权获取、创建和删除机密以及获取和更新 ServiceAccounts 的角色。

 kind: Role apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1 metadata: name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater rules: - apiGroups: [""] resources: ["secrets"] verbs: ["get", "create", "delete"] - apiGroups: [""] resources: ["serviceaccounts"] verbs: ["get", "patch"]

此配置的一个关键组件是k8sawsecrsecretupdater角色。 此角色是 Kubernetes 命名空间内授权的基础，允许 cronjob 对特定资源执行特定操作。

特别是， k8sawsecrsecretupdater角色具有以下权限：

  1. 它具有获取（ get ）、创建（ create ）和删除（ delete ） Secrets的权限。 这一点至关重要，因为 cronjob 需要能够创建和删除 AWS ECR 凭证，这些凭证作为秘密存储在 Kubernetes 中。
  2. 它具有获取（ get ）和更新（ patch ） ServiceAccounts 的权限。 为这些操作创建特定角色可确保 cronjob 具有完成其工作所需的权限，而无需授予它对不必要资源的访问权限。 这种方法符合最小特权原则，这是一种常见的安全实践，将对资源的访问限制为执行特定任务所必需的资源。 这有助于最大限度地减少可能的攻击的潜在影响。

    作业和 cronjob 使用的 ServiceAccount。

     apiVersion: v1 kind: ServiceAccount metadata: name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater

    创建秘密的工作。

     apiVersion: batch/v1 kind: Job metadata: name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater spec: backoffLimit: 4 template: spec: serviceAccountName: k8sawsecrsecretupdater restartPolicy: Never containers: - name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater image: ghcr.io/paranoiasystem/k8s-aws-ecr-secret-updater:latest imagePullPolicy: Always env: - name: AWS_ACCOUNT value: 'YourAwsAccountID' - name: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID value: YourAccessKeyID - name: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY value: YourSecretAccessKey - name: AWS_REGION value: YourRegion

    每 6 小时运行一次作业的 CronJob。

     apiVersion: batch/v1 kind: CronJob metadata: name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater spec: schedule: "0 */6 * * *" successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 3 failedJobsHistoryLimit: 1 jobTemplate: spec: template: spec: serviceAccountName: k8sawsecrsecretupdater restartPolicy: Never containers: - name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater image: ghcr.io/paranoiasystem/k8s-aws-ecr-secret-updater:latest imagePullPolicy: Always env: - name: AWS_ACCOUNT value: 'YourAwsAccountID' - name: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID value: YourAccess - name: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY value: YourSecretAccessKey - name: AWS_REGION value: YourRegion

    创建 Docker 镜像

    此 Docker 映像是根据以下 Dockerfile 构建的：

    在 Dockerfile 中，从一个 Alpine 基础镜像开始，安装必要的工具，包括 git、bash、curl、openssh、python3、py3-pip、py-cryptography、wget、curl、jq。 此外，还安装了 kubectl 和 AWSCLI 以分别与 Kubernetes 和 AWS 进行交互。

    随后，工作目录设置为 /scripts 并复制本地 /scripts 目录的内容。 最后，定义了一个 ENTRYPOINT，它在容器运行时启动 entrypoint.sh 脚本。

    脚本的执行

    当 cronjob 运行时，它会启动 Docker 映像中包含的 bash 脚本。 此脚本检查是否存在名为"regcred"的秘密。 如果它存在，它将删除它并创建一个新的。 如果它不存在，它会创建它。 下面是脚本：

     #!/bin/bash create_secret() { kubectl create secret docker-registry regcred \ --docker-server=${AWS_ACCOUNT}.dkr.ecr.${AWS_REGION}.amazonaws.com \ --docker-username=AWS \ --docker-password=$(aws ecr get-login-password --region ${AWS_REGION}) } # Check if the secret exists if kubectl get secret regcred; then # If it exists, delete it kubectl delete secret regcred # Create the secret again create_secret else # If it doesn't exist, create it create_secret fi

    在 Kubernetes 上安装 k8s-aws-ecr-secret-updater

    要在您的 Kubernetes 环境中使用k8s-aws-ecr-secret-updater ，您需要遵循一些简单的步骤。

    让我们首先将项目的 GitHub 存储库克隆到您的本地系统：

     git clone https://github.com/paranoiasystem/k8s-aws-ecr-secret-updater

    接下来，您需要打开并编辑存储库中的install.yaml文件。 在此文件中，您需要设置以下值：

    • 完成这些更改后，保存并退出 install.yaml 文件。

      现在，要在您的 Kubernetes 集群中安装k8s-aws-ecr-secret-updater ，您需要执行以下命令：

       kubectl apply -n <destination_namespace> -f install.yaml

      请记住将destination_namespace替换为要安装 cronjob 的 Kubernetes 命名空间。

      结论

      该工具消除了手动更新凭据的需要，从而节省了时间并降低了出错的风险。 我希望本文和我创建的工具可以帮助任何处理 Kubernetes 集群中管理 AWS ECR 凭证的类似问题的人。



原文:

In the course of my work with AWS ECR (Elastic Container Registry), I ran into a problem: The repository access key expires every six hours. Working with a non-AWS Kubernetes test cluster, I had to constantly update these credentials manually, a repetitive and tedious process.

From this experience came the idea to create a tool that automated this process: k8s-aws-ecr-secret-updater. This tool is a Kubernetes cronjob, designed to automatically update the AWS ECR repository access credentials.

Cronjob Configuration

The YAML code to create the cronjob consists of several parts, which I will now analyze piece by piece:

A Role that has permission to get, create, and delete secrets and get and update ServiceAccounts. 

kind: Role
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
metadata: name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater
rules:
- apiGroups: [""] resources: ["secrets"] verbs: ["get", "create", "delete"]
- apiGroups: [""] resources: ["serviceaccounts"] verbs: ["get", "patch"]

A key component of this configuration is the k8sawsecrsecretupdater role. This role is fundamental for authorization within the Kubernetes namespace, allowing the cronjob to perform specific operations on certain resources.

In particular, the k8sawsecrsecretupdater role has the following permissions:

  1. It has permissions to get (get), create (create), and delete (delete) Secrets. This is crucial because the cronjob needs to be able to create and delete AWS ECR credentials, which are stored as secrets in Kubernetes.
  2. It has permissions to get (get) and update (patch) ServiceAccounts. The cronjob needs to be able to manage service accounts in order to associate the AWS ECR credentials with the service that runs the cronjob.

Creating a specific role for these operations ensures that the cronjob has exactly the permissions it needs to do its job, without granting it access to unnecessary resources. This approach is in line with the principle of least privilege, a common security practice that limits access to resources only to what is strictly necessary to perform a specific task. This helps to minimize the potential impact of a possible attack.

ServiceAccount to be used by the job and cronjob. 

apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata: name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater

RoleBinding to associate the Role with the ServiceAccount. 

kind: RoleBinding
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
metadata: name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater
subjects: - kind: ServiceAccount name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater
roleRef: kind: Role name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io

Job that creates the secret. 

apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata: name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater
spec: backoffLimit: 4 template: spec: serviceAccountName: k8sawsecrsecretupdater restartPolicy: Never containers: - name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater image: ghcr.io/paranoiasystem/k8s-aws-ecr-secret-updater:latest imagePullPolicy: Always env: - name: AWS_ACCOUNT value: 'YourAwsAccountID' - name: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID value: YourAccessKeyID - name: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY value: YourSecretAccessKey - name: AWS_REGION value: YourRegion

CronJob that runs the Job every 6 hours. 

apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata: name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater
spec: schedule: "0 */6 * * *" successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 3 failedJobsHistoryLimit: 1 jobTemplate: spec: template: spec: serviceAccountName: k8sawsecrsecretupdater restartPolicy: Never containers: - name: k8sawsecrsecretupdater image: ghcr.io/paranoiasystem/k8s-aws-ecr-secret-updater:latest imagePullPolicy: Always env: - name: AWS_ACCOUNT value: 'YourAwsAccountID' - name: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID value: YourAccess - name: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY value: YourSecretAccessKey - name: AWS_REGION value: YourRegion

Creating the Docker Image

The cronjob uses a specific Docker image to perform its task. This Docker image is built from the following Dockerfile:

In the Dockerfile, starting from an Alpine base image, the necessary tools are installed, including git, bash, curl, openssh, python3, py3-pip, py-cryptography, wget, curl, jq. Also, kubectl and AWSCLI are installed for interaction with Kubernetes and AWS, respectively.

Subsequently, the working directory is set to /scripts and the contents of the local /scripts directory are copied. Finally, an ENTRYPOINT is defined that starts the entrypoint.sh script when the container is run.

Execution of the Script

When the cronjob is run, it starts the bash script contained in the Docker image. This script checks for the existence of a secret called "regcred". If it exists, it deletes it and creates a new one. If it doesn't exist, it creates it. Below is the script:

#!/bin/bash create_secret() { kubectl create secret docker-registry regcred \ --docker-server=${AWS_ACCOUNT}.dkr.ecr.${AWS_REGION}.amazonaws.com \ --docker-username=AWS \ --docker-password=$(aws ecr get-login-password --region ${AWS_REGION})
} # Check if the secret exists
if kubectl get secret regcred; then # If it exists, delete it kubectl delete secret regcred # Create the secret again create_secret
else # If it doesn't exist, create it create_secret
fi

Installing k8s-aws-ecr-secret-updater on Kubernetes

To use the k8s-aws-ecr-secret-updater in your Kubernetes environment, you need to follow some simple steps.

Let's start by cloning the project's GitHub repository onto your local system:

git clone https://github.com/paranoiasystem/k8s-aws-ecr-secret-updater

Next, you need to open and edit the install.yaml file present in the repository. In this file, you will need to set the following values:

  • AWS_ACCOUNT: your AWS account ID.
  • AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: your AWS access key.
  • AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: your AWS secret access key.
  • AWS_REGION: the AWS region where your ECR is located.

Once you have made these changes, save and exit the install.yaml file.

Now, to install the k8s-aws-ecr-secret-updater in your Kubernetes cluster, you need to execute the following command:

kubectl apply -n <destination_namespace> -f install.yaml

Remember to replace destination_namespace with the Kubernetes namespace where you want to install the cronjob.

Conclusions

This tool eliminates the need for manual updating of credentials, saving time and reducing the risk of errors. I hope this article and the tool I created can be of help to anyone dealing with a similar issue in managing AWS ECR credentials in a Kubernetes cluster.
item.author: paranoiasystem

item.title: 字节跳动开源其云原生数据仓库：ByConity | ByteDance Open Sources Its Cloud Native Data Warehouse : ByConity
item.description:
它采用计算存储分离架构，提供计算存储分离、弹性扩展、租户资源隔离、数据读写强一致性等基本特性。 通过利用列存储、矢量化执行、MPP 执行和查询优化等流行的 OLAP 引擎的优化，ByConity 提供了卓越的读写性能。

随着业务的增长，数据量显着增加以满足庞大的用户群。 然而，ClickHouse的Shared-Nothing架构，每个节点独立运行，不共享存储资源，在使用过程中带来了一定的挑战。 下面是遇到的一些问题：

扩展限制：

由于 ClickHouse 中计算和存储资源的紧耦合，系统扩展成本较高，并且涉及数据迁移。 这阻碍了实时按需可扩展性，导致资源利用效率低下。

多租户和共享集群环境：

由于读写操作是在同一个节点上进行的，它们经常会相互干扰，影响整体性能。

性能限制：

针对这些痛点，字节跳动对ClickHouse进行了架构升级。 该项目于 2023 年 1 月发布 Beta 版本后，于 2023 年 5 月底正式向公众开放。


图 1：字节跳动 ClickHouse 使用情况

相反，它使用分布式存储对整个集群中的所有数据采用统一的管理方法。 这种转变导致了无状态的计算节点，通过利用分布式存储的可扩展性和计算节点的无状态特性来实现动态扩展和收缩。 存储计算分离

这种架构保证了读写操作互不影响，计算资源和存储资源可以独立按需扩缩容，保证了资源的高效利用。 图2：ByConity存储计算分离实现多租户隔离

资源隔离

这可以防止租户之间的干扰或影响，促进数据隐私和高效的多租户支持。

弹性缩放

这种灵活性确保了资源的高效利用，并使系统能够适应不断变化的工作负载需求。

强数据一致性

这确保了数据始终是最新的，并消除了读写操作之间的任何不一致，保证了数据的完整性和准确性。

高性能

这些优化提高了读写性能，从而实现更快、更高效的数据处理和分析。

服务访问层：服务访问层，以 ByConity Server 为代表，处理客户端数据和服务访问。
  • 计算层：计算层由多个计算组组成，每个虚拟仓库（VW）作为一个计算组。
  • 数据存储层：数据存储层采用HDFS、S3等分布式文件系统。


    • 图 3：ByConity 的技术架构

    在本节中，我们将通过一条SQL的完整生命周期来考察ByConity的工作原理以及ByConity各组件的交互过程。


    图 4：ByConity 内部组件交互图

    图 4 描述了 ByConity 组件的交互图。 图中，虚线表示一个SQL查询的流入，双向箭头表示组件交互，单向箭头表示数据处理并输出到客户端。 让我们来探讨一下 ByConity 中每个组件在 SQL 查询的整个生命周期中的交互过程。

    第一阶段：查询请求

    客户端向服务器提交查询请求。 服务器首先执行解析，随后通过查询分析器和优化器对查询进行分析和优化，生成高效的可执行计划。 为了访问存储在分布式键值 (KV) 存储中的所需元数据，ByConity 利用FoundationDB并从Catalog中读取元数据。

    阶段 2：计划调度

    调度器访问资源管理器以获取可用的计算资源，并确定将查询任务调度到哪些节点执行。

    第三阶段：查询执行

    查询请求在ByConity 的 Workers上执行。 然后服务端聚合多个Worker的计算结果返回给客户端。

    此外，ByConity 包括两个主要组件：时间戳 Oracle 和 Daemon Manager。 时间戳预言机支持事务处理，而守护进程管理器管理和调度后续任务。

    主要元件库

    为了更好的理解ByConity的工作原理，我们先来看看ByConity的主要组件：

    元数据管理

    它支持完整的事务语义 (ACID)。 此外，我们设计了具有灵活架构的目录服务器，允许后端存储系统的可插拔性。 目前支持苹果开源的FoundationDB，未来有可能扩展到其他后端存储系统。

    查询优化器

    查询优化器在数据库系统的性能中起着至关重要的作用。 一个设计良好的优化器可以显着提升查询性能，尤其是在复杂的查询场景下，可以实现数倍到数百倍的性能提升。 这包括对连接重新排序、外部连接重新排序、连接/聚合重新排序、公用表表达式 (CTE)、物化视图、动态过滤器下推、魔术集优化和其他基于成本的技术的支持。 此外，它还集成了分布式规划的财产执行。

    查询调度

    缓存感知调度侧重于计算和存储分离的场景。 其目标是最大化缓存利用率并最小化冷读。 该策略旨在将任务调度到具有相应数据缓存的节点，使计算能够利用缓存并提高读写性能。 另外，考虑到系统的动态伸缩，缓存感知调度力求在计算组拓扑发生变化时，尽量减少缓存失效对查询性能的影响。

  • 资源感知调度分析整个集群中计算组内不同节点的资源使用情况。 它执行有针对性的调度以优化资源利用率。 此外，资源感知调度结合了流量控制机制，以确保合理利用资源并防止过载造成的负面影响，例如系统停机。

    • 计算组

    每个租户可以利用独立的计算组实现多租户隔离，支持读写分离。 计算组可按需动态扩缩容，保证资源高效利用。 在资源利用率较低的时期，可以采用资源共享的方式，将计算组分配给其他租户，从而最大限度地提高资源利用率，降低成本。

    虚拟文件系统

    虚拟文件系统模块作为数据读写的中间层。 虚拟文件系统提供了统一的文件系统抽象，屏蔽了底层不同的后端实现。 便于扩展，支持多种存储系统，如HDFS或对象存储。

    缓存加速

    在计算存储分离架构下，缓存加速在元数据和数据两个维度上进行。 在元数据维度，ByConity将Table和Partition信息缓存在服务器端（ByConity Server）的内存中。 在数据维度，缓存加速发生在计算组内的Worker端。 这种分层缓存机制同时使用内存和磁盘，以标记集合作为缓存粒度。 这些缓存策略有效地提高了查询速度和性能。

    如何部署 Byconity

    欢迎社区开发者使用它们并向我们提交问题：

    其中，新存储引擎的开发、对更多数据类型的支持以及与其他数据管理工具的集成是一些重要的关注领域。 我们列出了以下方向，并在 Github 上创建了一个问题： github.com/ByConity/ByConity/issues/26 ，邀请社区加入我们讨论共同开发：

    • 性能改进：ByConity 旨在通过各种优化来提高性能。 这包括利用索引进行加速，例如 Skip-index 优化，支持新的 Zorder-index 和倒排索引。 查询优化器的不断增强和分布式缓存机制的实现也是性能改进工作的一部分。
    • 稳定性改进：这里有两个方面。
      • 一是支持更多维度的资源隔离。 二是丰富指标，提升可观测性和问题诊断能力，保障用户稳定可靠的体验。
    • 企业级功能增强：ByConity旨在引入更细粒度的权限控制，完善备份、恢复、数据加密等与数据安全相关的功能，并继续探索数据深度压缩技术以节省存储成本。
    • 生态系统兼容性改进：ByConity 计划扩展其与各种存储系统的兼容性，包括 S3 和 TOS 等流行的对象存储解决方案。 它计划增强整体兼容性和集成能力，促进与其他工具和框架的无缝集成。 此外，它旨在支持数据湖联合查询，实现与 Hudi、Iceberg 等技术的交互。

    与社区合作

    自Beta版本发布以来，ByConity得到了包括华为、电子云、战心战力、天翼云、唯品会、传音控股在内的众多企业开发者的支持。 这些组织通过在各自的环境中部署 ByConity、进行 TPC-DS 验证以及在其业务场景中进行测试，积极做出贡献。 结果令人鼓舞，他们的反馈为改进提供了宝贵的见解，我们对此深表感谢。

    我们很高兴收到社区合作伙伴共同建设的想法和意愿。 我们已经启动了与华为终端云的联合开发工作。 我们的协作努力将集中在各个领域，例如 Kerberos 身份验证、ORC 支持以及与 S3 存储的集成。

    如果您有兴趣加入我们的社区并参与 ByConity 的开发，我们邀请您访问我们的 GitHub 存储库github.com/ByConity/ByConity 。 您可以找到有关我们正在进行的项目的更多信息和详细信息，贡献您的想法，并与我们合作以进一步增强 ByConity。 要参与，只需扫描下面提供的二维码即可加入我们的Discord或在Twitter上关注我们。

    概括

    综上所述，ByConity是一个开源的云原生数据仓库，具有读写分离、弹性伸缩、租户资源隔离、数据读写强一致性等特性。 它采用计算存储分离架构，并利用主流OLAP引擎的优化来提供出色的读写性能。 随着 ByConity 的不断发展和完善，它的目标是成为未来云原生数据仓库的关键工具。



    原文:

    ByConity is an open-source cloud-native data warehouse developed by ByteDance. It utilizes a computing-storage separation architecture and offers various essential features, including the separation of computing and storage, elastic scalability, tenant resource isolation, and strong consistency in data reading and writing. By leveraging optimizations from popular OLAP engines like column storage, vectorized execution, MPP execution, and query optimization, ByConity delivers exceptional read and write performance.

    History of ByConity

    The origins of ByConity can be traced back to 2018 when ByteDance initially implemented ClickHouse for internal use. As the business grew, the data volume increased significantly to cater to a large user base. However, ClickHouse's Shared-Nothing architecture, where each node operates independently without sharing storage resources, posed certain challenges during its usage. Here are some of the issues encountered:

    Expansion limitations:

    Due to the tight coupling of computing and storage resources in ClickHouse, scaling the system incurred higher costs and involved data migration. This prevented real-time on-demand scalability, resulting in inefficient resource utilization.

    Multi-tenancy and shared cluster environment:

    ClickHouse's tightly coupled architecture led to interactions among multiple tenants in a shared cluster environment. Since reading and writing operations were performed on the same node, they often interfered with each other, impacting overall performance.

    Performance limitations:

    ClickHouse's support for complex queries, such as multi-table join operations, was not optimal, which hindered the system's ability to handle such queries efficiently.

    To address these pain points, ByteDance undertook an architectural upgrade of ClickHouse. In 2020, we initiated the ByConity project internally. After releasing the Beta version in January 2023, the project was officially made available to the public at the end of May 2023.


    Figure 1: ByteDance ClickHouse Usage

    Features of ByConity

    ByConity implements a computing and storage separation architecture that transforms the original local management of computing and storage on individual nodes. Instead, it adopts a unified management approach for all data across the entire cluster using distributed storage. This transformation results in stateless computing nodes, enabling dynamic expansion and contraction by leveraging the scalability of distributed storage and the stateless nature of computing nodes. ByConity offers several crucial features that enhance its functionality and performance:

    Storage-Computing Separation

    One of the critical advantages of ByConity is its storage-computing separation architecture, which enables read-write separation and elastic scaling. This architecture ensures that read and write operations do not affect each other, and computing resources and storage resources can be independently expanded and contracted on demand, ensuring efficient resource utilization. ByConity also supports multi-tenant resource isolation, making it suitable for multi-tenant environments.


    Figure 2: ByConity storage-computing separation to achieve multi-tenant isolation

    Resource Isolation

    ByConity provides resource isolation, ensuring that different tenants have separate and independent resources. This prevents interference or impact between tenants, promoting data privacy and efficient multi-tenancy support.

    Elastic Scaling

    ByConity supports elastic expansion and contraction, allowing for real-time and on-demand scaling of computing resources. This flexibility ensures efficient resource utilization and enables the system to adapt to changing workload requirements.

    Strong Data Consistency

    ByConity ensures strong consistency of data read and write operations. This ensures that data is always up-to-date and eliminates any inconsistencies between read and write operations, guaranteeing data integrity and accuracy.

    High Performance

    ByConity incorporates optimization techniques from mainstream OLAP engines, such as column storage, vectorized execution, MPP execution, and query optimization. These optimizations enhance read and write performance, enabling faster and more efficient data processing and analysis.

    Technical Architecture of ByConity

    ByConity follows a three-layer architecture consisting of:

    1. Service access layer: The service access layer, represented by ByConity Server, handles client data and service access.
    2. Computing layer: The computing layer comprises multiple computing groups, where each Virtual Warehouse (VW) functions as a computing group.
    3. Data storage layer: The data storage layer utilizes distributed file systems like HDFS and S3.


    Figure 3: ByConity's technical architecture

    Working Principle of ByConity

    ByConity is a powerful open-source cloud-native data warehouse that adopts a storage-computing separation architecture. In this section, we will examine the working principle of ByConity and the interaction process of each component of ByConity through the complete life cycle of a SQL.


    Figure 4: ByConity internal component interaction diagram

    Figure 4 depicts the interaction diagram of ByConity's components. In the figure, the dotted line represents the inflow of a SQL query, the double-headed arrow indicates component interaction, and the one-way arrow represents data processing and output to the client. Let's explore the interaction process of each component in ByConity throughout the complete lifecycle of a SQL query.

    ByConity's working principle can be divided into three stages:

    Stage 1: Query Request

    The client submits a Query request to the server. The server initially performs parsing and subsequently analyzes and optimizes the query through the Query Analyzer and Optimizer to generate an efficient executable plan. To access the required metadata, which is stored in a distributed key-value (KV) store, ByConity leverages FoundationDB and reads the metadata from the Catalog.

    Stage 2: Plan Scheduling

    ByConity passes the optimized executable plan to the Plan Scheduler component. The scheduler accesses the Resource Manager to obtain available computing resources and determines which nodes to schedule the query tasks for execution.

    Stage 3: Query Execution

    The Query request is executed on ByConity's Workers. The Workers read data from the underlying Cloud Storage and perform computations by establishing a Pipeline. The server then aggregates the calculation results from multiple Workers and returns them to the client.

    Additionally, ByConity includes two main components: Time-stamp Oracle and Daemon Manager. The time-stamp oracle supports transaction processing, while the daemon manager manages and schedules subsequent tasks.

    Main Component Library

    To better understand the working principle of ByConity, let's take a look at the main components of ByConity:

    Metadata Management

    ByConity offers a highly available and high-performance metadata read and write service called the Catalog Server. It supports complete transaction semantics (ACID). Furthermore, we have designed the Catalog Server with a flexible architecture, allowing for the pluggability of backend storage systems. Currently, we support Apple's open-source FoundationDB, and there is potential for extending support to other backend storage systems in the future.

    Query Optimizer

    The query optimizer plays a crucial role in the performance of a database system. A well-designed optimizer can significantly enhance query performance, particularly in complex query scenarios, where it can achieve performance improvements ranging from several times to hundreds of times. ByConity's self-developed optimizer focuses on improving optimization capabilities through two main approaches:

    • RBO (Rule-Based Optimization): This capability encompasses various optimizations such as column pruning, partition pruning, expression simplification, subquery dissociation, predicate pushdown, redundant operator elimination, Outer-Join to Inner-Join conversion, operator pushdown storage, distributed operator splitting, and other heuristic optimization techniques.

    • CBO (Cost-Based Optimization): ByConity's optimizer also includes cost-based optimization capabilities. This includes support for join reorder, outer-join reorder, join/agg reorder, common table expressions (CTE), materialized views, dynamic filter push-down, magic set optimization, and other cost-based techniques. Additionally, it integrates property enforcement for distributed planning.

    Query Scheduling

    ByConity currently supports two query scheduling strategies: Cache-aware scheduling and Resource-aware scheduling.

    • The cache-aware scheduling focuses on scenarios where computing and storage are separated. Its objective is to maximize cache utilization and minimize cold reads. This strategy aims to schedule tasks to nodes that have corresponding data caches, enabling computations to leverage the cache and improve read and write performance. Additionally, considering the dynamic expansion and contraction of the system, cache-aware scheduling strives to minimize the impact of cache failure on query performance when the computing group's topology changes.

    • Resource-aware scheduling analyzes the resource usage of different nodes within the computing group across the entire cluster. It performs targeted scheduling to optimize resource utilization. Moreover, resource-aware scheduling incorporates flow control mechanisms to ensure rational resource utilization and prevent negative effects caused by overload, such as system downtime.

    Computing Group

    ByConity enables different tenants to utilize distinct computing resources, as depicted in Figure 4. With ByConity's architecture, implementing features like multi-tenant isolation and read-write separation becomes straightforward. Each tenant can leverage separate computing groups to achieve multi-tenant isolation and support read-write separation. The computing groups can be dynamically expanded and contracted on-demand, ensuring efficient resource utilization. During periods of low resource utilization, resource sharing can be employed, allowing computing groups to be allocated to other tenants to maximize resource utilization and minimize costs.

    Virtual File System

    The virtual file system module serves as an intermediary layer for data reading and writing. ByConity has optimized this module to provide a "storage as a service" capability to other modules. The virtual file system offers a unified file system abstraction, shielding the underlying different back-end implementations. It facilitates easy expansion and supports multiple storage systems, such as HDFS or object storage.

    Cache Acceleration

    ByConity utilizes caching to accelerate query processing. Under the computing-storage separation architecture, cache acceleration is performed in both the metadata and data dimensions. In the metadata dimension, ByConity caches Table and Partition information in the memory of the server-side (ByConity Server). In the data dimension, cache acceleration occurs on the Worker side within the computing group. This hierarchical caching mechanism utilizes both memory and disk, with Mark collection serving as the cache granularity. These caching strategies effectively enhance query speed and performance.

    How to Deploy Byconity

    ByConity currently supports four acquisition and deployment modes. Community developers are welcome to use them and submit issues to us:

    ByConity's Future Open-Source Plan

    ByConity includes several key milestones in its open-source community roadmap through 2023. These milestones are designed to enhance ByConity's functionality, performance, and ease of use. Among them, the development of new storage engines, support for more data types, and integration with other data management tools are some important areas of focus. We have listed the following directions, and created an issue on Github: github.com/ByConity/ByConity/issues/26, inviting the community to join us to discuss co-development:

    • Performance improvement: ByConity aims to boost performance through various optimizations. This includes leveraging indexes for acceleration, such as Skip-index optimization, support for new Zorder-index and inverted indexes. ByConity will also focus on the construction and acceleration of external indexes, as well as the automatic recommendation and conversion of indexes. Continuous enhancements to the query optimizer and the implementation of a distributed caching mechanism are also part of the performance improvement efforts.
    • Stability improvement: There are two aspects here.
      • One is to support resource isolation in more dimensions. ByConity is committed to improving stability by extending resource isolation capabilities in multiple dimensions, thereby providing better multi-tenant support.
      • The second direction is to enrich metrics and improve observability and problem diagnosis capabilities, ensuring a stable and reliable experience for users.
    • Enterprise-level feature enhancements: ByConity aims to introduce finer-grained authority control, improve data security-related functions such as backup, recovery, and data encryption and continue to explore techniques for deep compression of data to save storage costs.
    • Ecosystem compatibility improvement: ByConity plans to expand its compatibility with various storage systems, including popular object storage solutions like S3 and TOS. It plans to enhance the overall compatibility and integration capabilities, facilitating seamless integration with other tools and frameworks. Moreover, it aims to support data lake federation queries, enabling interaction with technologies like Hudi, Iceberg, and more.

    Working with the Community

    Since the release of the Beta version, ByConity has received support from numerous enterprise developers, including Huawei, Electronic Cloud, Zhanxinzhanli, Tianyi Cloud, Vipshop, and Transsion Holdings. These organizations have actively contributed by deploying ByConity in their respective environments, undergoing TPC-DS verification, and conducting tests in their business scenarios. The results have been promising, and their feedback has provided valuable insights for improvement, which we greatly appreciate.

    We are delighted to receive the ideas and willingness of community partners to build together. We have already initiated joint development efforts with Huawei Terminal Cloud. Our collaborative endeavors will focus on various areas, such as Kerberos authentication, ORC support, and integration with S3 storage.

    If you are interested in joining our community and participating in the development of ByConity, we invite you to visit our GitHub repository at github.com/ByConity/ByConity. You can find more information and details about our ongoing projects, contribute your ideas, and collaborate with us to further enhance ByConity. To get involved, simply scan the QR code provided below to join our Discord or follow us on Twitter.

    Summary

    In summary, ByConity is an open source cloud-native data warehouse that offers features such as read-write separation, elastic expansion and contraction, tenant resource isolation, and strong data read and write consistency. It utilizes a computing-storage separation architecture and leverages optimizations from mainstream OLAP engines to deliver excellent read and write performance. As ByConity continues to evolve and improve, it aims to become a key tool for cloud-native data warehousing in the future.
item.author: vinijaiswal

item.title: 使用 JWT 登录并注册 Auth | Login and Sign up Auth using JWT
item.description:
维沙尔辛德

发表于 6 月 21 日

这是我正在构建的整个 Journaling Web 项目的一部分，目的是获得 NodeJS 和 MongoDB 方面的经验。 我用过 NodeJS、Express、Mongoose 和 EJS（嵌入式 JavaScript）

我们通过以下命令使用 npm 安装所需的包。

npm i express bcrypt dotenv mongoose cookie-parser ejs jsonwebtoken

然后我们安装 nodemon 作为 Dev Dependency 以在每次保存后保持我们的服务器运行

在命令行上，您只需键入npm run dev ，您的服务器就会实时运行。

我们为注册、登录、注销和主页编写路由。

创建一个名为“models”的目录并将 user.js 保存在其中。
 // user.js const mongoose = require('mongoose'); const userSchema = new mongoose.Schema({ email : { type : String, required : true, unique : true, validate : { validator : async function (value) { const user = await this.constructor.findOne({email : value}) return !user; }, message : 'Email already exists', }, }, password : { type : String, required : true, } }) module.exports = mongoose.model("User", userSchema)
  • 我已经进行了电子邮件验证，它将在注册期间检查电子邮件是否已被使用。 字段的所有必需选项均已写入。
  • 我们创建了一个用户模式模型并导出 user.js 以在路由中使用它。 我们可以使用此行导入 user.js const User = require('../models/user.js');

按名称路由创建目录并创建 login.js 和 signup.js 文件。 首先我们导入所有需要的包。

 const express = require('express'); const router = express.Router(); const bcrypt = require('bcrypt'); const User = require('../models/user.js');

我们为我们的注册路线写了两个请求。 获取和发布。

 router.get('/', (req, res) => { res.render('signup', { title: 'Sign Up', emailError: null, passError : null }); });
 router.post('/', async (req, res) => { const { email, password } = req.body; try { const existingUser = await User.findOne({ email }); if (existingUser) { return res.render('signup', { emailError: 'Email already exists', title: 'Sign Up', passError : null }) } if(password.length < 8) { return res.render('signup', { title: 'Sign Up', passError: 'Password must be at least 8 characters long', emailError : null }) } const salt = await bcrypt.genSalt(10); const hashedPassword = await bcrypt.hash(password, salt); const newUser = new User({ email, password: hashedPassword }); await newUser.save(); res.redirect('/login') // res.send('Welcome to Journalling'); } catch (error) { console.error(error); res.redirect('/signup'); } });
  • 正如我们在 router.get 方法中看到的，我们正在使用在 views/signup.ejs 中创建的 ejs 渲染我们的视图。 在这里我们写下我们的观点并渲染它。 我们可以使用我在 app.js 中编写的这两行告诉我们的服务器使用视图引擎

app.set('view engine', 'ejs');

  • 视图引擎负责呈现动态模板并生成要作为响应发送给客户端的 HTML。 行 app.use(express.static('public')); 是 Express.js 应用程序中的一个中间件函数，它提供来自“公共”目录的静态文件。

这是我的 signup.ejs

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <title>Journalling | <%= title %></title> <link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="/styles.css"> </head> <body> <div class="container"> <h1>Sign up</h1> <form action="/signup" method="POST"> <label for="email">Email</label> <input type="email" id="email" name="email" required placeholder="Email"> <% if (emailError) { %> <p class="error-message"><%= emailError %></p> <% } %> <% if (passError) { %> <p class="error-message"><%= passError %></p> <% } %> <label for="password">Password</label> <input type="password" id="password" name="password" required> <button type="submit">Sign up</button> </form> </div> </body> </html>
  • 我们设计了一个带有 POST 请求的表单，这意味着我们在字段中输入的任何内容都将作为 req.body 发送。

  • 我使用 emailError 和 passError 变量来验证电子邮件和密码。 这同样适用于电子邮件。

  • 让我们转到发布请求函数体。 我们将电子邮件和密码作为我们的 req.body 将其保存在我们的数据库中。 我写了电子邮件和密码验证的逻辑

    我们将通过 User.findOne({}) 检查现有用户。 我们已经注释了await关键字来处理异步行为。


    然后放置一个 if 条件来检查existingUser是否为真，然后我们将使用错误消息呈现相同的注册。 类似的逻辑适用于密码，如果密码长度小于 8，它将呈现相同的页面并显示错误消息。

  • 我们在开始时安装了 bcrypt，它用于密码散列，以散列格式存储密码是一种很好的做法，可以保护用户免受攻击。 然后我们在密码中加盐。 盐是在散列之前添加到密码的随机值。 我们将凭据存储到我们的数据库中，并将用户重定向到登录以访问受保护的路由。

基本思路是，我们将比较用户的密码和登录时输入的密码，并进行验证以检查电子邮件是否存在于我们的数据库中，如果是，则以与我在注册时相同的方式显示错误消息路线。

  • 现在进入JWT，用于Auth(Authenticaion and Authorization)。
    您可以通过此链接了解 JWT 1 2 3

  • 在登录或注册（或注册）期间生成令牌的决定有其优点和缺点。 如果我们在注册期间生成令牌，就会有无缝的登录体验。 如果我们在登录期间这样做，我们可以提高安全性。 这是我的 login.js

    •  const express = require('express'); const router = express.Router(); const bcrypt = require('bcrypt'); const User = require('../models/user.js'); const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken') require('dotenv').config(); router.get('/', (req, res) => { res.render('login', { title: 'Login', error : null }); }); router.post('/', async (req,res) => { const { email, password } = req.body; try { // Find the user by email const user = await User.findOne({email}); // If user is not found, display an error message if(!user) { return res.render('login',{title : 'Login', error : 'Invalid Email or Password'}) } // Compare the entered password with the stored hashed password const isPasswordValid = await bcrypt.compare(password, user.password); // if password is invalid, display an error message if (!isPasswordValid) { return res.render('login', { title: 'Login', error: 'Invalid email or password' }); } token = jwt.sign({id: user._id,email:user.email},process.env.ACCESS_TOKEN, {expiresIn: '30m'}) res.cookie('jwt',token, {httpOnly : true, secure : true}); res.redirect('/dashboard'); } catch (error) { console.error(error); res.redirect('/login'); } }) module.exports = router;
    • 您需要一个秘密令牌，用于生成您的 JWT 令牌。 您可以使用此命令在节点控制台上使用加密库require('crypto').randomBytes(64).toString('hex')
    • 这将生成一个随机令牌，然后将其放入 .env 文件中。 并访问它。 注意：这是一个密钥，可用于生成您的 JWT 令牌，因此确保其安全至关重要。 如果您要将存储库上传到 Github，请不要忘记将 .env 文件添加到您的 .gitignore 文件中。
    • 我们通过 jwt.sign() 生成 JWT 令牌，它将包含三个参数，我们要存储的数据，我们之前谈到的密钥和到期时间（可选）。 当且仅当令牌有效且存在时，我们将此令牌存储在客户端浏览器中并重定向到受保护的路由。

    要在记录后访问路由，我们必须设置一个中间件函数，它会在我们每次请求受保护资源时运行。 这是检查令牌是否有效且存在的中间件功能

    const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken'); require('dotenv').config(); const authenticateToken= (req,res,next) => { const token = req.cookies.jwt; // check json exists and is verified if(token) { jwt.verify(token, process.env.ACCESS_TOKEN, (err, decodedToken) => { if(err) { console.log(err.message); res.status(403).redirect('/login') // If the token is provided but invalid (eg, expired or tampered with): Status code 403 (Forbidden) can be used. This indicates that the user is authenticated but does not have permission to access the requested resource. In this case, you can redirect the user to the login page or display an error message. } else { next(); } }) } else { // token is missing or not provided // If the token is missing or not provided: Status code 401 (Unauthorized) can be used. This indicates that the user is not authenticated and should be redirected to the login page. res.status(401).redirect('/login') } } module.exports = authenticateToken
    • 使用req.cookies.jwt从请求 cookie 中提取令牌。
    • 该代码检查令牌是否存在。 如果是，它将继续使用jwt.verify验证令牌。 如果令牌验证成功，则调用 next() 函数以继续执行下一个中间件或路由处理程序。 这表明用户已通过身份验证并有权访问所请求的资源。

    我们不能直接从服务器端删除 cookie，而是创建一个空白 cookie 并将其过期时间设置为 1 毫秒或仅 0（立即）。
  • 从客户端（也是浏览器），我们可以从开发者工具中删除 cookie，但这不是一个好的用户体验，因此实现了一个按钮，可以为我们删除 cookie。
  • 删除 cookie 最终撤销令牌将使用户退出他们的会话。 这是 logout.js 的代码片段
    • const router = require('express').Router(); router.get('/logout',(req,res) =>{ res.cookie('jwt','',{maxAge : 1}) res.redirect('/'); }) module.exports = router;

    我们有所有必需的路线。 现在我们将它全部导入到我们的主 app.js 中，它将运行我们的整个应用程序。 这是 app.js
  • 为 app.js 导入了所有必需的包
    • const express = require('express'); const mongoose = require('mongoose'); const cookieParser = require('cookie-parser')
     const app = express(); // Creates an instance of our Express Application
    • 下面包含了我们上面写的所有Routes
    • 当且仅当 auth 中间件运行时，protectedRoute 才会执行，因此它确保用户已登录。
     const loginRoute = require('./routes/login.js'); const signupRoute = require('./routes/signup.js'); const protectedRoute = require('./routes/dashboard.js') const logoutRoute = require('./routes/logout.js');
     mongoose.connect('mongodb://localhost:27017/database_name'); // connect to our mongoDB Database using Mongoose
    • 在这里，我们使用我们的中间件功能和所需的解析器，如 json、cookie。
    • 这允许您使用 req.body 在路由处理程序中访问提交的表单数据。 例如，如果您有一个带有名为 email 的输入字段的表单，您可以在路由处理程序中访问它的值作为 req.body.email。
     app.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: true })); app.use(express.json()); app.use(cookieParser()) // Register view engine app.set('view engine', 'ejs'); // Static files app.use(express.static('public')); app.get('/', (req, res) => { res.render('index.ejs'); }); // this is our main/index route where our app starts. and we are rendering index.ejs(home page) // Mount the login routes app.use('/login', loginRoute); // Mount the signup routes app.use('/signup', signupRoute); app.use('/',logoutRoute) app.use('/dashboard',protectedRoute); app.listen(3000); // is used to start the Express application and make it listen for incoming HTTP requests on port 3000.

    我正在使用这个博客来记录我在项目中学到的一切。 如果有人能从中受益，我会很高兴。
    此外，我还没有在这里完成刷新令牌，这是处理令牌的更好方法，以防万一令牌被盗，我可能会在稍后对其进行编辑。

    感谢您阅读！

    一旦暂停，vishalsh 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

    解除暂停后，vishalsh 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，vishalsh 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 vishalsh 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    笔记：

    感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 vishalsh：

    降低 vishalsh 的所有帖子的可见度

    报告其他不当行为

    取消标记 vishalsh 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Vishal Shinde

    Posted on Jun 21

     

    This is a part of my whole Journaling Web Project which I am building to gain experience in NodeJS and MongoDB. I have used NodeJS, Express, Mongoose and EJS(Embedded JavaScript)

    We install required packages using npm by the following command.

    npm i express bcrypt dotenv mongoose cookie-parser ejs jsonwebtoken

    Then we install nodemon as a Dev Dependency for keeping our server live after every save

    npm i --save-dev nodemon In your package.json file,you change your scripts to this

    "dev": "nodemon app.js"


    On the command line you just type npm run dev and your server will run live.

    We write Routes for Sign up, Login, Logout and Home Page.

    1. Creating a User Schema using Mongoose

    • Make a directory called "models" and keep user.js in it. 
    // user.js
const mongoose = require('mongoose');
const userSchema = new mongoose.Schema({
    email : {
        type : String,
        required : true,
        unique : true,
        validate : {
            validator : async function (value) {
                const user = await this.constructor.findOne({email : value})
                return !user;
            },
            message : 'Email already exists',
        },
    },
    password : {
        type : String,
        required : true,
        }
    })
    module.exports = mongoose.model("User", userSchema)
    • I have taken a Email Validation which will check during Sign up if the Email is already taken. All the required options for Fields is written.
    • We create a model of our User Schema and export user.js to use it in routes. We can import user.js using this line const User = require('../models/user.js');

    2. Writing the Signup Route.

    Make directory by name routes and create login.js and signup.js files. First we import all the required packages.

    const express = require('express');
const router = express.Router();
const bcrypt = require('bcrypt');
const User = require('../models/user.js');

    We write two requests for our sign up route. GET and POST.

    router.get('/', (req, res) => {
  res.render('signup', { title: 'Sign Up', emailError: null, passError : null });
});

    router.post('/', async (req, res) => {
  const { email, password } = req.body;
  try {
    const existingUser = await User.findOne({ email });
    if (existingUser) {
      return res.render('signup', {
        emailError: 'Email already exists',
        title: 'Sign Up',
        passError : null
      }) }
    if(password.length < 8) {
      return res.render('signup', {
        title: 'Sign Up',
        passError: 'Password must be at least 8 characters long',
        emailError : null
      })
    }
    const salt = await bcrypt.genSalt(10);
    const hashedPassword = await bcrypt.hash(password, salt);
    const newUser = new User({ email, password: hashedPassword });
    await newUser.save();
    res.redirect('/login')
    // res.send('Welcome to Journalling');
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(error);
    res.redirect('/signup');
  }
});
    • As we can see in router.get method, we are rendering our view using ejs which is created in views/signup.ejs. here we write our views and render it. We can tell our Server to use view Engines using this two lines which I have written in app.js

    app.set('view engine', 'ejs');

    • A view engine is responsible for rendering dynamic templates and generating HTML to be sent as a response to the client. EJS is one of the popular view engines available for Express.js.

    app.use(express.static('public'));

    • The line app.use(express.static('public')); is a middleware function in an Express.js application that serves static files from the "public" directory.

    Here is my signup.ejs

    <!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <title>Journalling | <%= title %></title>
  <link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="/styles.css">
</head>
<body>
  <div class="container">
    <h1>Sign up</h1>
    <form action="/signup" method="POST">
      <label for="email">Email</label>
      <input type="email" id="email" name="email" required placeholder="Email">
      <% if (emailError) { %>
        <p class="error-message"><%= emailError %></p>
        <% } %>

        <% if (passError) { %>
          <p class="error-message"><%= passError %></p>
          <% } %>
      <label for="password">Password</label>
      <input type="password" id="password" name="password" required>
      <button type="submit">Sign up</button>
    </form>
  </div>
</body>
</html>
    • We have designed a form with POST request it means whatever we put in fields will send as req.body.

    • I have used emailError and passError variables for Email and Password validation. emailError to check if Email already exists and passError to take atleast 8 characters as a password.

    • <% %> is for any control flow, and it does not give any output display to the page.

    • <%= %> gives output to the page in this case I have written passErrror inside a paragraph tag so whenever a user will put less than 8 characters of password, they will get a error message. same applies for the Email.

    • Let's move on to the post request function body. We take email and password as our req.body to save it in our database. I wrote the logic for Email and Password Validation

      We will check for Existing user by User.findOne({}). We have annotated await keyword to handle asynchronous behavior.


      then put a if-condition to check whether existingUser is true then we will render the same signup with a error message. Similar logic applies for password, if the password length is less than 8, it will render the same page with an error message.

    • We installed bcrypt in the start, it is used for password hashing, it is a good practice to store password in a hashed format to protect users from attackers. then we add salt to our password. A salt is a random value that is added to the password before hashing. The purpose of the salt is to introduce additional randomness and uniqueness to each hashed password.

      We store the credentials to our Database and redirect the user to login to access the protected routes.

    3. Writing the Login Route.

    • The Basic idea is, we will be comparing the passwords of the user and password inputted during login and put a validation to check if email exists in our database or not, if yes, display an error message in the same way I did in the signup route.

    • Now enters JWT, which is used for Auth(Authenticataion and Authorization).
      You can learn about JWT at this links 1 2 3

    • The Decision to generate tokens during login or signup(or register) has its pros and cons. If we generate token During Signup, there is seamless login experience. and if we do it durign login, we could have enchanced security. Here is my login.js

    const express = require('express');
const router = express.Router();
const bcrypt = require('bcrypt');
const User = require('../models/user.js');
const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken')
require('dotenv').config();

router.get('/', (req, res) => {
  res.render('login', { title: 'Login', error : null });
});

router.post('/', async (req,res) => {
    const { email, password } = req.body;
    try {
        // Find the user by email
        const user = await User.findOne({email});

        // If user is not found, display an error message
        if(!user) {
            return res.render('login',{title : 'Login', error : 'Invalid Email or Password'})
        }
        // Compare the entered password with the stored hashed password
        const isPasswordValid = await bcrypt.compare(password, user.password);

        // if password is invalid, display an error message
        if (!isPasswordValid) {
            return res.render('login', { title: 'Login', error: 'Invalid email or password' });
        }
        token = jwt.sign({id: user._id,email:user.email},process.env.ACCESS_TOKEN, {expiresIn: '30m'})
        res.cookie('jwt',token, {httpOnly : true, secure : true});
        res.redirect('/dashboard');
    } catch (error) {
        console.error(error);
        res.redirect('/login');
    }
})
module.exports = router;
    • You need a secret token which will used to generate your JWT Token. you can use crypto library on node console using this command require('crypto').randomBytes(64).toString('hex')
    • This will generate a random token which you will then put it in your .env file. and access it. Note: It is a secret key which can be used to generate your JWT token so it's crucial to keep it safe. If you will be uploading your repository to Github then don't forget to add .env file to your .gitignore file.
    • We generate JWT token by jwt.sign(), which will contain three parameters, the data we want to store, a secret key we talked earlier and a expiry(optional). We store this token in Client-side Browser and redirect to a protected route if and only if token is valid and exists.

    4. Writing a auth middleware

    To access the routes after logging, we have to setup a middleware function which will run everytime we request a protected resource. This is the middleware function which check if token is valid and exists

    const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken');
require('dotenv').config();

const authenticateToken= (req,res,next) => {
  const token = req.cookies.jwt;

  // check json exists and is verified
  if(token) {
      jwt.verify(token, process.env.ACCESS_TOKEN, (err, decodedToken) => {
          if(err) {
              console.log(err.message);
              res.status(403).redirect('/login')    
      // If the token is provided but invalid (e.g., expired or tampered with): Status code 403 (Forbidden) can be used. This indicates that the user is authenticated but does not have permission to access the requested resource. In this case, you can redirect the user to the login page or display an error message.
          } else {
              next();
            }
      })
  } else {
  // token is missing or not provided
  // If the token is missing or not provided: Status code 401 (Unauthorized) can be used. This indicates that the user is not authenticated and should be redirected to the login page.
      res.status(401).redirect('/login')
  }
}
module.exports = authenticateToken
    • The token is extracted from the request cookies using req.cookies.jwt.
    • The code checks if the token exists. If it does, it proceeds to verify the token using jwt.verify. The jwt.verify function verifies the token's authenticity and decodes its contents.
    • If the token is successfully verified, the next() function is called to proceed to the next middleware or route handler. This indicates that the user is authenticated and has permission to access the requested resource.

    5. Writing the Logout Route.

    • We cannot delete cookies from server-side directly, instead we created a blank cookie and set its expiry to 1ms or just 0(immediately).
    • From client-side(also Browser), we can remove cookies from Developer tools but that's not a good UX, so implemented a button which will delete cookies for us.
    • Deleting the cookies eventually revoking the token will log out the user out of their session. Here is the Code Snippet for logout.js 
    const router = require('express').Router();
router.get('/logout',(req,res) =>{
    res.cookie('jwt','',{maxAge : 1})
    res.redirect('/');
})
module.exports = router;

    6. Final Part.

    • We have all required Routes. Now we import it all in our main app.js which will run our entire application. Here is the app.js
    • Imported all the Required Packages for app.js 
    const express = require('express');
const mongoose = require('mongoose');
const cookieParser = require('cookie-parser')

    const app = express(); // Creates an instance of our Express Application
    • The following contains all the Routes we wrote above
    • protectedRoute will execute if and only if auth middleware runs, so it ensure the user is logged in. 
    const loginRoute = require('./routes/login.js');
const signupRoute = require('./routes/signup.js');
const protectedRoute = require('./routes/dashboard.js')
const logoutRoute = require('./routes/logout.js');

    mongoose.connect('mongodb://localhost:27017/database_name'); // connect to our mongoDB Database using Mongoose
    • Here, we use our middleware functions and required parsers like json, cookie.
    • urlencoded is used to parse URL-encoded data in the request body. This allows you to access the submitted form data in your route handlers using req.body. For example, if you have a form with an input field named email,you can access its value in a route handler as req.body.email. 
    app.use(express.urlencoded({ extended: true }));
app.use(express.json());
app.use(cookieParser())
// Register view engine
app.set('view engine', 'ejs');

// Static files
app.use(express.static('public'));

app.get('/', (req, res) => {
  res.render('index.ejs'); 
}); // this is our main/index route where our app starts. and we are rendering index.ejs(home page)

// Mount the login routes
app.use('/login', loginRoute);

// Mount the signup routes
app.use('/signup', signupRoute);

app.use('/',logoutRoute)
app.use('/dashboard',protectedRoute);
app.listen(3000); //  is used to start the Express application and make it listen for incoming HTTP requests on port 3000.

    PS : This is my First ever Tech Blog, there could by too many mistakes so Open for Suggestions. I am using this blog thing to take note of whatever I learnt during projects. I would be glad if someone could benefit from it.
    Moreover, I haven't done refresh tokens here which is more better way to handle Tokens incase if a token ever gets stolen, I might edit it later on.

    THANK YOU FOR READING!

    Rafael Levi Costa - Jun 6

    John Owolabi Idogun - Jun 5

    Micael Levi L. C. - Jun 17

    Once suspended, vishalsh will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, vishalsh will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by vishalsh will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If vishalsh is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Note:

    Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag vishalsh:

    Make all posts by vishalsh less visible

    vishalsh consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging vishalsh will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: IHM – USABILIDADE E FOCO NO USUÁRIO | IHM – USABILIDADE E FOCO NO USUÁRIO
item.description:
安娜门德斯

发表于 6 月 21 日

青少年和成年人可以使用 tecnológicas que facilitam atividades e oferecem inúmeras plataformas de ensino 和 lazer dentre tantas outras finalidades。 一个重要的重要时刻的使用问题：在您的设备上是否会干扰您的使用体验？

方法：Trata-se de uma pesquisa descritiva，na qual seu objetivo é descrever as characterísticas e as experiências dos estudos realizados na disciplina Projeto Integrador。 结论： Com a globalização da tecnologia veio também a necessidade de acompanhar esse processo evolutivo。 一项技术已在当地实施，并在 mesmo substituir certos 过程中进行。 重要的是，它是一个重要的发展过程，它可以让你在实践中使用更多的东西，因为它是一种新的方式，它可以连接到互联网，也可以是你的通信和信息。 满意..

参考资料： CARDOSO，Gabriel Cordeiro； 载于：第三十三届巴西通信大会，2010 年，南卡希亚斯。 描述：Usabilidade。 技术。

一旦暂停，anamendes 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

一旦解除暂停，anamendes 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，anamendes 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 anamendes 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 anamendes 的方法：

使 anamendes 的所有帖子不那么显眼

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 anamendes 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ana Mendes

Posted on Jun 21

Introdução: A importância de estar conectado, acompanhando atualizações que somente a internet proporciona faz parte do cotidiano da grande maioria dos jovens, que nos dias atuais fazem da internet uma grande ferramenta de comunicação e aprendizado (CARDOSO, 2019). Mas é uma realidade que não chega a um número considerável de brasileiros. Este artigo busca trazer a discussão sobre a usabilidade em usuários com pouca experiência de uso. Até que ponto se torna fácil a utilização e que tipo de abordagem seria adequada para usuários que possuem dificuldades em ter acesso à aplicativos e afins, usando a usabilidade como um ponto atrativo para novos usuários. O mercado tecnológico vem crescendo consideravelmente, conectando mundos e expandindo o conhecimento. Dentre jovens e adultos é comum o uso de ferramentas tecnológicas que facilitam atividades e oferecem inúmeras plataformas de ensino e lazer dentre tantas outras finalidades. Em contramão, há um público que não tem acesso à essa tecnologia. Em pesquisa do IBGE em 2016, aponta que 65 milhões de brasileiros não acessaram a internet. Desses 65, 24 milhões não usaram a internet por falta de conhecimento. A pesquisa ainda mostra que a dificuldade de uso e a falta de interesse estavam próximas, somando um total de 37,6 % na lista dos principais motivos para não utilizarem a internet e suas ferramentas tecnológicas. A questão da usabilidade entra nesse momento de grande importância: até onde pode interferir ou ajudar quando se trata de usuários com pouca experiência?

O termo usabilidade carrega um conceito chave dentro da IHM, buscando um projeto de sistemas onde sejam fáceis de aprender e usar, além de envolver pontos importantes, como fatores psicológicos, organizacionais, ergonômicos e sociais. Fatores esses que precisam serem desenvolvidos e traduzidos em formato de ferramentas e técnicas que ajudem no desempenho do projeto. Essas ferramentas são utilizadas para alcançar a eficiência, efetividade e segurança na interação. São pontos importantes no desenvolvimento, eles definirão como as pessoas irão operar e fazerem uso de computadores e****fetivamente. Dentre os processos importantes dentro da Usabilidade, destacam-se a facilidade de aprendizado, a eficiência do sistema, a facilidade de ser lembrado, o nível de erros e por fim a satisfação subjetiva.

Objetivos: Relatar a análise do estudo acadêmico realizado sobre o tema Usabilidade e Interface Homem Máquina. Métodos: Trata-se de uma pesquisa descritiva, na qual seu objetivo é descrever as características e as experiências dos estudos realizados na disciplina Projeto Integrador. Resultados: Em relação ao foco no usuário: Nos primórdios da computação, somente pessoas com conhecimento avançado na área tinham acesso à computadores, cenário esse que mudou por completo nos dias atuais, onde estar conectado faz parte da rotina de inúmeros brasileiros. Com essa mudança e a popularização do uso de computadores e smartphones , ocasionou na necessidade do uso de uma metodologia de desenvolvimento centrada no usuário, onde o foco são suas necessidades, ou seja, criar um produto baseado inicialmente no usuário e não em sua tecnologia, colocar em prioridade a experiência do usuário (UX). O foco no usuário tem a necessidade de levantar vários pontos de pesquisa e avaliação, observar o grau de afetividade, facilidade de aprendizagem, flexibilidade e deixar explícito a finalidade e utilidade do produto. Além de apresentar uma adequação aceitável entre as funções oferecidas pelo sistema e atender as necessidades e requisitos do usuário, levando em consideração sempre as características da tarefa e de quem irá realizá-la. Em relação à compreensão da necessidade do usuário: Desenvolver um sistema voltado para as necessidades e satisfação do usuário consiste em passos e processos onde envolvem cognição, memorização e a atenção de quem irá utilizar. Saber as necessidades do usuário e o que ele almeja é o primeiro passo para entender melhor o que irá desenvolver para suprir suas necessidades, onde podem entrar importantes processos como o de impacto da interface e ferramentas de fácil memorização. Para chegar a todos estes processos é necessário uma pesquisa de campo e selecionar um público alvo, onde serão avaliadas o que os usuários necessitam e o que o produto final irá oferecer. Em usuários com baixo nível de escolaridade, o foco na interação é uma importante alternativa para atrair novos usuários. A interação pode ser dividida em 4 perspectivas importantes, são elas: Perspectiva de sistema, onde o usuário deve aprender a interagir com o sistema; Perspectiva de discurso, onde o sistema assume o papel do humano e cria uma comunicação (raciocina, guarda informações e afins); Perspectiva de ferramenta, onde o sistema auxilia o usuário realizar suas atividades; Perspectiva de mídia, é emergente e consiste nos sistemas computacionais que conectam pessoas via internet.

Conclusão: Com a globalização da tecnologia veio também a necessidade de acompanhar esse processo evolutivo. A tecnologia veio para acrescentar praticidade e até mesmo substituir certos processos. Nos dias atuais dificilmente não estamos conectados, seja no trabalho, em hospitais, bancos e em momentos de lazer. A importância em acompanhar essa evolução é não somente poder usufruir da praticidade mas como poder fazer parte da atualidade e dessa nova geração de pessoas conectadas que utilizam a internet como meio de comunicação e informação. Um exemplo a ser citado é o processo de aprendizado, processo esse que envolve uma busca de informações que não são tão simples, e a tecnologia vem como um meio de facilitar essa busca e dentre tantas outras funções. A proposta é o foco em desenvolver sistemas e aplicativos que sejam de fácil compreensão do usuário, levando em consideração suas limitações e o cenário em que se encontra e o que ele espera do produto. Visando a inclusão de novos usuários que faziam parte da porcentagem que não utilizavam de sistemas e aplicativos apenas por sentirem dificuldades. Desenvolvendo de forma direta e simples, utilizando símbolos padronizados e de fácil memorização e aprendizagem, um sistema que já seja idealizado baseado em um público alvo escolhido, focando em suas necessidades e expectativas e podendo criar um ambiente onde se possa desenvolver de forma centralizada e assim chegar em sua funcionalidade, oferecendo comunicabilidade e segurança de uso em conjunto a uma interface elaborada de forma a tornar a experiência do usuário eficiente e


satisfatória..

Referências: CARDOSO, Gabriel Cordeiro; GONÇALVES, Berenice Santos, A tecnologia como mediadora dos processos de aprendizagem: o exemplo de um ambiente online de aprendizagem social. In: XXXIII Congresso Brasileiro de Ciência da Comunicação, 2010, Caxias do Sul. ANAIS XXXIII Congresso Brasileiro de Ciência da Comunicação, 2010.

Descritores: Usabilidade. Experiência do usuário. Tecnologia.

Ravindra Singh Rathor - Jun 21

Calcular Porcentaje - Jun 21

Arif Patel Preston Dubai - Jun 21

Once unsuspended, anamendes will be able to comment and publish posts again.

If anamendes is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
